Hey everybody, my name is Russell Brunson. I wanna welcome you to our funnilology course. Today I'm gonna be talking about the new art and science of funnel building. And we're gonna be going over a lot of the core concepts about how to build a funnel, how to build an offer, how to have value ladders, ascension funnels, and then all the different types of funnels throughout the value ladder. What are the lead funnels? What are the unboxing funnels? What are the presentation funnels? What are the high ticket funnels? And it's gonna be a lot of fun. Now my guess is that during this training, there's different people listening to this. And for some of you guys, some of these concepts are gonna be review. Some of you guys, everything I say is gonna be brand new and you're gonna be freaking out the first time around. I wanna recommend that if you are someone who's advanced and you're looking at this, pay attention because there's a lot of new things I'm gonna be introducing. There'll be similar frameworks you're familiar with, but there's gonna be new stuff weaved in through every single presentation as well. I think that everyone's gonna get a ton of value whether you've been doing this for a decade or decade and a half like me, or if you're a brand new beginner who's trying to figure out how in the world can I grow my business online with sales funnels? For both of you guys, this course is called Funnelology is gonna help you. I call it the new art and science of funnel building because the first time I ever taught this, I spent the first 10 years of my career online doing funnel building. I launched tons of different companies. We had weight loss companies, we had diabetic neuropathy companies, we had speed reading companies, we had baby sign language, potato gun DVDs, we had all sorts of products and services where I was building funnels and doing this. And about five or six years ago, I decided to start teaching people about this. And I wrote my very first book ever called Dotcom Secrets, which was the first real introduction to sales funnels and how people can use funnels to grow companies. And about the same time we launched the book, we launched our software company ClickFunnels, and hand in hand, that became the guidebook to help people actually use funnels inside their business. And for the last five years, it's been really fun watching people grow huge companies off of that foundation. Now, what's been fun is right now, my third book is coming out really, really soon. And I had a really short window to have a chance to actually rewrite the Dotcom Secrets book. And it's funny, the first edition was 58,000 words. I deleted 30,000 words, and then the book ended up being, I think, 94,000 words. So it's a lot bigger. So even though some of the concepts in the book and some of the concepts you may have heard before, there's a lot of new stuff weaved into it that I think you guys are gonna love and you're gonna get obsessed about. If you geek out like me on funnels, you're gonna love everything we're talking about today. So that's what this new presentation is really based on, a lot of the core methodologies that are taught in the new Dotcom Secrets book. And it should give you guys a really good foundation to prepare yourself for whatever business you're in. So what I recommend is while you're going through this training course, to sit back, take out a pad of paper, take out some notes. And as you're learning the concepts, I want to take notes on it, but at the same time, start thinking about it in the lens of your business. Like how would this work in my business? How would this piece work? And start thinking about those things because you start visualizing and seeing how this would fit in your business. The more you visualize it, the more it becomes real to you and the more likely you are to actually use that as a tool. I don't know about you, but I always, my entire career, I struggled in school. I think a lot of times, because I was sitting there, I listened to the teacher talking and telling me stories. And I'd be trying to pay attention, I'd take notes, but I'm like, I have no context of how this is valuable in my life. So because of that, we go right in one ear and right out the other one just as fast, right? And so for me, education got exciting when I had something to actually apply it to. Since I had a business and I learned something, I'm like, huh, that's interesting. And I tried it and it worked. All of a sudden I was like, oh my gosh, I want to learn everything I can. I started reading books and buying courses and going to seminars and events. And everything I could do to dump in ideas into this side of my brain so I could test them over here and the ones that worked and the ones that stuck, we started using over and over and over again. And through that process, I was able to build companies that have done hundreds of millions of dollars in sales. And so my goal for you is that I don't want this stuff just to bounce off, you know, going one ear and out the other one. I want it to be meaningful to you. I want you to be able to use it. So as I'm telling you about, think about, how could I apply that to the business that I have or the idea I have or maybe I would consult them for somebody else's business. How would I apply these things to help grow their company? And if you do that, these things will mean a lot more to you and remember them better and hopefully they'll serve you at a much higher level, okay? So I'm gonna jump right in. And again, there's gonna be a lot of foundational stuff today. Be going over the secret formula and value ladders today and then tomorrow we're gonna go deeper into each of the different phases of the value ladder. What are the funnels on the bottom of the funnel, the value ladder? What are the funnels in the second tier and the third tier and the fourth tier of the value ladder? And we're going very, very deep into detail of what the funnels are, what they look like, what does page one look like in page two and page three? So we'll get to that tomorrow, but today I really want you guys to understand the foundational stuff. Because if you don't have these foundations, it's really hard to build a funnel. You do have them, building funnels is the easiest part of the entire equation. So this first session, I call this the secret formula. And the secret formula is a really simple process that when I wrote the .com secrets book initially, it kind of mapped out this entire funnel building process in like a couple really, really quick images. And so if you look at how it works, the first part of the secret formula is who. Who is the person you actually want to serve? I'm gonna dive deeper into this, but who is the person you want to serve? That's number one. The second question is where are those people? If I know I want to serve this type of person, where are they already congregating? So here's a lake and there's some fish. So that's the second thing, it's like, where are my people? And the third part of the value ladder is now that I know who they are, I know where they are, what is the bait that I'm gonna use? There's a fishing pole to go and capture these fish. What's the bait I'm gonna use? Okay, and then the last step here is after I've caught this person, I've caught this bait, I brought him in, then where am I taking him? This is the little mountain here. Where is the summit that I'm trying to take them to? What's the result I'm trying to get for my dream customer? And one of the big updates that we made to the Dockham Secrets book is you'll see when you guys have a chance to read it in the near future, is that this step we kind of identify, like what is actually bait? And so if you look at what bait is inside of bait, it's kind of a three-step process we can go through today as well. It's hook and then story and then offer. And this kind of encompasses what your bait is. Your bait is your hook, your story, and your offer. So that's kind of what the secret formula is. It's a very simple process, but it really encompasses all of business. Who do I wanna serve? Where are those people at? What can I use to get them? And then after I get them, how can I actually serve them? Okay, that's what the secret formula looks like. So if you look here on my slides, this is kind of a doodle of that. So who, where, bait, and result. Don't go a little bit deeper into each of these aspects because sure, most of you guys just heard that. You're like, okay, cool, I got it. It's like, no, like each of these things, they could and should do an entire college semester on each of these different aspects because they're so vitally important to what you're doing. Okay, so the first one I go to is who. Who is your dream customer? Who is that person that you wanna serve? Okay, now I always tell people this, that if you're not careful, what most entrepreneurs do is they get excited about a product, and they create a product, they start selling it, and they never think about who it is they actually wanna serve. They're thinking about this idea they have and this product and wanna launch it. Okay, and I got started on my entrepreneur journey, that's exactly what I did. I got excited about a product, I created it, I built it, I started selling it, and I didn't think once about who I wanted to sell it to. I just started selling it, and some people started buying it, I got excited, more people bought it, and one day, like three years down the road, I woke up, and I remember laying in bed, sitting there, and I was so miserable. I was making really good money. I had all the things you would think were like, what gives you a perfect lifestyle, and I'm laying there, and I was like, I wish that I had a boss so that he would fire me, and I wouldn't have to go in the office today. And I started sitting there thinking, why do I feel this way? And I realized the reason why is because I had created some really good products that attracted the wrong hoos, the wrong people into my world. And I wasn't obsessed with my customer, I didn't like him, I didn't even wanna see him. I always thought about this, that if I was to go on a cruise, or go hang out with somebody, or go to an event, surrounded by my dream customers, would I be like, hey guys, let's go hang out? Or I'd be like, oh man, I gotta go hide. And I realized I wanted to hide, I didn't love the people I was serving. And because of that, I was miserable. And it wasn't until I hit rock bottom, my business was collapsing and failing, I was miserable, that I sat back, and I remember I had a friend, Evan Pagans, and he has no Evan. He had a training out there talking about your dream customer avatar. And I remember hearing that, and I was like, I never thought about it. I created a product and I sell it, that's what entrepreneurs do. And he was like, no, no, no, no. You gotta figure out who is it you actually wanna serve? Who is your dream customer avatar? That's where you start. And he gave the suggestion, he said, go to Google Images and actually find images of the people who your dream avatar could be. And I thought, first of all, I remember thinking of that, which is like the cheesiest thing in the world. And I was like, all right, I'm gonna do it. So I went to Google Images and I said, okay, who's my dream customer? What do they look like? And I went and I first started, I wanted to find two avatars. I wanted to find a male avatar and a female avatar. So I went there, I went to Google Images, I typed in the name. So I said, okay, I'm gonna name my avatar, I'm gonna call her Julie. And I said, okay, no, what do I know about Julie? Well, Julie, if she's my dream customer, she's successful, she's driven. I said she has a message she wants to share with the world. She values her own personal growth over money. She already has her business to eight or nine figure, or eight, excuse me, to six figure years. She just wants to grow to the next level. I type those things in and I'll send all these faces popped into Google Images. I'm scrolling through and eventually I found this face right here. Now I don't know who she actually is, but this is my Julie. This is Julie who popped out of Google Images for me. I was like, oh my gosh, she looks like someone I wanna hang out with. I was going through to Veta Bay, hey Julie, what's up? Like we should hang out and be friends. So I took a picture of this off Google Images. I put it in a frame and it had on my wall. I said, this is Julie. This is my dream customer that I wanna serve. Okay, and that was my first person. The next person I said, I said, well, who's my male counterpart? Like who would I wanna serve on the male side? I remember I typed, I said, okay, I'm gonna call this guy Mike. So I called him Mike, typed in Google. I typed in a bunch of things. I typed in former athlete. He's changed someone's life in a small way and wants to share it with more. He values growth over money. He already has a business to five figure year, but wants to grow to six and seven and beyond. Typed all that stuff into Google Images and boom, this picture popped out of Mike. And I was like, Mike looks awesome. I would totally go hang out with this guy. We'd have so much fun. We talked about marketing and business and sports. I would like, I would love to hang out with Mike. So I took his picture, I printed it out, put it on my wall, and I had my Mike and my Julie. Now, I tell you the story because I was kind of almost, doing it initially as a joke, sounds like this is not like something that's actually gonna work, right? But I did it, put them on the wall, and I started thinking about it. When I was trying to create different products, I look at them and I'm like, is this something that Mike and Julie would want? Is this something that my dream customer would actually want? If I said this in an ad, would that get them to come towards me or would that repel and make them push away from me? I started thinking about these things and it shifted my mindset on so many things from how we created products to the different ads we made and all sorts of things like that. Now, kind of a funny backstory. Some of you guys know I've ran an inner circle mastermind program for the last six or seven years. And this year, I actually put it on pause for a little while. My kids are gonna be starting high school next year and a bunch of other things and I just wanted, and not as much wanted, but I needed, I needed and wanted. I needed both, but I needed to block out time so I wanted to spend more time with my kids and so something had to go. And I had this amazing mastermind program that's making me tons of money and I went to all my people and I paused it. I said, I'm gonna pause it for a couple of years just so I can get my kids through high school and all those kind of things. And it was a sad emotional thing. This is actually my inner circle, one of my inner circle members a couple of times a year and it was really sad. And I remember as I was sitting out there and I was talking to them the last day, the last meeting, and I looked out and they were all there and I told them about this story. I said, you know what's crazy? I said, five or six years ago, I built out this customer avatar and I started, I went through and I pulled up Julie. I started talking about who Julie was and I said, who here in this room is a Julie? And literally all the women's hands raised up. They're like, that's me. And then sitting right there was Julie's story in and Julie's like, I've had so many people who read the DotCom Seekers book and asked if this Julie was me. Because it's like feeds, like fits her to a T. And I was like, and look, I said, because I put that out five years ago, all the decisions and choices I made led to a room full of Julie's. And I talked about the mic and I explained the mic person. I said, who here is the mic? And all the men raised their hand. I said, isn't it fascinating? This little tweak that I made five or six years ago just to define who do I wanna serve and then figuring out the products and services that they would want as opposed to me being like, this would make me money. This would be a good thing I could sell. I shifted it to focus on them first. And because of that little choice, that little hinge swung this huge door and now five or six years later, I had a mastermind with over 100 people in it, paying $50,000 a year to be part of it. It was all filled with my dream customers. People I would wanna hang out with anyway. And so that's the power. So that's the very first step is who? Becoming super clear on who it is you wanna serve. If you're listening to me right now and you're like, I hate my customers, it's probably because you did what I did. It's okay to change, especially now that at the beginning of a year, now is the best time in the world to change. In fact, I made this decision. It was right after New Year's and there was a video that Tony Robbins and his wife Sage put out, they were in Fiji. And I remember that they were sitting there talking and Tony Robbins said, you know, we can change anytime we want all year long. A lot of people during New Year's, they give themselves permission to actually change. And he said, I challenge you that if you're in a relationship you don't like, you should change it. So if you're in a business you don't like, you should change it. If you're in a whatever, you name it, if you're in a body you're not happy with, you should change it. And I remember sitting there and this is when I made this decision of like, I'm gonna change my customers. Because Tony gave me permission. So I wanna give you guys permission. Just because you've gone a year, two years, five years, ten years down in business, does not mean you can't shift it. You can't change it. I did and on the other side of that, so much more happiness and fulfillment, it'll be worth it to you. Okay, so that's the more one is, who is your dream customer? His second question I was like, where are these people actually congregating? Okay, it becomes really easy when you realize like exactly who this person is. You know, okay, this is what the person is, what they do, what they like. And the better you get to know who this person is, the better you're gonna be at figuring out where are they actually congregating? Okay, like where do they spend their time? I made a list last night of just some things that are thinking about, about where. Okay, what are the top websites your dream customers already go to every single day? Okay, what forums or message boards they participate in? What are the Facebook groups they participate in? Who are the influencers they follow on Facebook and Instagram? What podcasts they listen to? What are the email newsletters they're subscribed to? What blogs they read? What channels they follow on YouTube? What keywords they're searching for on Google to find information? Okay, this is where your dream customers are congregating, where they're at. Okay, so your job as a marketer is to figure out, I gotta find out where these people are. Okay, it's funny, I just wrote this book, Traffic Secrets. And whenever I talk to people about traffic, they're like, they think it's this magic mystery of like, okay, well, how do I, you know, how do I do it? Like, what are the keywords? What are the things, and they're always like worried about all the algorithms. I'm like, look, traffic is really simple when you think about it. It's just figuring out who your dream customers and figuring out where they're congregating. Okay, one of my favorite stories, I'm sure most of you guys have heard me tell a story before, but when I was in high school and college, I was a wrestler, I wrestled in Utah growing up. I was a state champ there, wrestled the year of bringing me to university, and they cut the wrestling programs. I came to Boise State, and I had my last four years here at Boise State. And I remember, as much as I love my people, my wrestlers, we're not always the smartest group in any bunch. And because most of us were struggling to make grades, they made it mandatory for every single wrestler, you had to go to study hall every single night. So every single night, practice would get done, we'd all go home, we'd eat, and we'd drive over to study hall, and all the wrestlers would be in study hall because we were all struggling in school. And so we're sitting there doing homework, and I remember every single day doing this, and I remember it was probably two or three months into my sophomore year, and I'm sitting there, and I'm trying to do my homework, and then I'm thinking about wrestling, thinking about my match coming up, and thinking about other things, and there was a website that me and all of the wrestlers always congregated at, and it was the mat.com. Matt hasn't, it's kind of shrunk in the last few years, and there's, for my wrestlers, there's bigger ones out there that are better, but the mat.com was the place when I was in college. And so I remember I'd go to mat.com, and I get sucked into the message boards, and talking about this wrestler versus this wrestler, and thing after thing, and that was my place, those were my people. We hung out, we had a good time, and that's where we spent all of our time, is all congregating here in this one spot. And I remember kind of a funny story one day, as I was in there, I had to go to the bathroom, I stood up, I started walking out, and I was looking over, I started looking at the other wrestlers, and I noticed that our 133 pounder was on the mat.com, our heavyweight was on the mat.com, our 197 pounder, and I was looking, I'm like, oh my gosh, all of the wrestlers in Boise, in Study Hall, are literally in this one spot right now, talking about wrestling. I'm speaking, I'm like, I'm sure it's not just Boise State, I bet you every single college campus in the country, the wrestlers are in Study Hall right now, and we're all on the exact same website. And it's not just happening in college, it's happening in high school, it's happening for people that have already graduated, it's happening in other countries. Like the entire world, everyone who cares about wrestling, is all congregating in this one spot. So my question for you is, if you're selling a wrestling product, where do you go to throw your bait in? You just find out where are the wrestlers congregating, and you throw out your bait right there. I remember when I was in the mat.com, I would see there'd be ads on the side for the Grammy system wrestling thing, and Ken Chirtau's wrestling camps, and the Sioux plays dummies, all the ads were in there all the time, they're all people throwing their hooks in, trying to grab people out of this pond. So same thing's true for you. So you get a clear picture of who this person is, and then you figure out where they congregating. If you don't know where this is at, it means you gotta start getting deeper into your dream customer. You gotta start figuring out what podcasts they listen to. If you're not listening to the same podcasts they are, you're gonna struggle to speak to them, because you're not gonna know the language that they're already listening to. You're not gonna hear what other people are saying to them. So you gotta find out what the podcast my dream customer's listening to, and you should be listening. What are the Facebook groups that are on? You need to get inside those groups and be part of them, participate. You gotta become part of these communities, and that's gonna help you understand how to come in here and throw your bait in. All right, so that takes us now to number three. So who's your dream customer? Where are they congregating? Now what is the bait? The bait we're gonna throw in. Now we found this congregation, we gotta throw something in here to try to capture them, try to grab them. In the past I always said just throw out bait, but in the new version of the Dotcom secret book I talk about how bait is actually this concept called hook story offer. We have this thing, we throw our bait in, we have a hook. So if you think about it, they're in this forum, they're in this community, and I visualize this. Here's this person, this is your dream customer, they're hanging out in this community, and they're on their, this is the phone, they're on their phone, and they're seeing all these, all of them, they're scrolling, they're reading things, they're commenting, they're seeing stuff, and you're coming here, and you're throwing this hook in to try to grab their attention just for a second. Okay, so the first part of the process is you grab the hook to grab them, and we're gonna go deeper in this, you grab their hook, okay, and if you do it right, you get the tension just for a second, then you have this little window of opportunity now that you've got their attention, you have this window where you have a chance to tell them a story, and the goal of the story is to build a relationship and connection with you, okay, and then to increase the perceived value of whatever it is you're about to sell to them. Okay, so let me say it again, so the goal, the hook grabs their attention just long enough you can tell them a story, the story builds a connection with you and the person, okay, and then gives you the ability to increase the perceived value of the thing you're gonna offer them, and then you make them an offer, okay, so it's hook, yeah, hook, story, offer, this is the framework, I'm gonna go deeper in this here in a second, we hook them, tell them a story, and we make them an offer, okay, and that's how you get somebody from this community to be able to get their attention so you can actually start serving them, okay, now it's interesting, I'm gonna show you guys this right here, so this was the first bait, the first thing that we put out there, it was specifically to grab my dream customer, okay, this was my hook to grab Mike and Julie, so prior to this, so she has no, before I had this 108 split test book, most of the things I was selling, like how to get rich quick on the internet, how to make money on eBay, how to make money doing that, there are all these things that were like, they were attracting the wrong kind of person, wasn't attracting entrepreneurs, they were attracting who wanted to get rich quick, right, which those are the customers I didn't like that I didn't want to spend time with, okay, so I wrote this book 108 split test winners, and it was interesting, I took my existing customer list and I promoted it, I'm like, hey, I wrote this new book 108 split test winners, and on my, on 150, 200,000 subscribers ahead of time, the amount of people that bought this, so maybe a dozen, okay, so this bait I put out there, what did it do, I put this bait out here, and the wrong customers came and looked at it, said 108 split test, what does that even mean, okay, and it repelled them, like, I don't, like that sounds like a lot of work, so that it repelled them and it went away, okay, but the right people, my dream customer said, 108 split test, like, oh my gosh, if they're an entrepreneur, they're like, these are Russell's split tests, simple tweaks you can make to your funnel, like that bait attracted my dream customer, the mics and the julees of the communities that we were putting this offer out, they heard it and they're like, oh my gosh, like that's the thing I need, that's what I need to do, and they raised their hand, they came and they bought it, okay, what happened was interesting is that my huge list of all these business opportunity seekers who wanted to get rich quick on the internet, they stopped responding to my offers, okay, eventually they all kind of faded away and they disappeared, and also this new breed of entrepreneurs, the people that wanted to work hard, that believed in growth, they wanted to figure out the next level, those people saw this hook and it attracted them, they said, that's amazing, they came, they came, they bought the hook, okay, they saw me make an offer, tell them a story, like, let me tell you a story about this book, I told them the story of the book, like, oh my gosh, this is amazing, I must buy it, I need this thing, maybe an offer, go buy the book and boom, it happened, hook story offer, I started getting my, I started grabbing my dream customers out of this place right here and started coming into my world, and that literally is what shifted my whole future, my whole, everything since then, shifted because of that, because I knew exactly who my customers were, I knew where they were at, and I created the bait that would attract the dream customers and repel the people I didn't want to work with, does that make sense? Okay, so bait's the next thing here, okay, so the question for you to start asking is, what's the bait you can create to attract your dream customer? And the sexier the bait, the more likely is someone's gonna come to you, okay, if I go back when, it says, change your bait, change your customer, okay, if you don't like customers coming in, change your bait, keep changing until it's like, oh, these are my people, these are the dream, these are the people I want to actually spend time with, okay, change your bait, change your customer, okay, so start thinking through what's the bait you create, you could create, okay, you can grab your dream people, okay, now I want to go deeper here, I want to take this step in the framework here with bait, I want to zoom into this part right here, I want to go deeper on hook story offer, because this is one of the core frameworks that I talk about, not only in the dot com secrets book, but in the new expert secrets and the traffic secrets book, we go deep into this because it really is the core fundamental framework of almost everything we're doing here in funnels, is understanding that, so I'm gonna go a little deeper here, so we go into it deep, okay, so number one, we go through hook, the story, and the offer, okay, I kind of told this before, the hook, the gold hook is to grab their attention, the story is to build a relationship with you and then increase the perceived value thing you're about to offer, and then the offer is actually the offer, and what's interesting is, if you're struggling to make money online, if something isn't working in one of your funnels, it's always either the hook, the story, or the offer, okay, last year's Funnel Hacking Lab, I talked about this on stage, I told people over and over, I said, if you were to hire me and pay me $100,000 for a one day consult, and you came in front of me, you said, nothing's working, my funnel's not working, what's wrong, I would look at it, I would look at your ads, page one, page two, page three, page four, and I'd say, okay, let me look at your ads, it's either you hook your story or your offer, so let's figure out which one it is, and then I go to your Latin page, okay, it's either you hook your story or your offer, let's figure out which one it is, and we go to the next page, and that's either you hook your story or your offer, and that would be it, and you'd be like, Russell, I thought you were this amazing consultant, I'm like, yeah, I only got three things I look at, you hook your story or your offer, like this is the core fundamental principle of all of marketing, okay, so if you miss this, you're missing everything, so that's why it's so important, why I keep drilling in people's heads over and over and over again, I want to re-drill into your head, that's why it's gone, I go so deep into it, in all three of the books, because it really is, it really is the piece that if you can master this, it gets really easy, in fact, Stacey Martino, I'm gonna talk about Stacey and Paul here in a little bit, some of my favorite students here, they've been in my inner circle for the last couple years, and they're amazing, but after Stacey heard me say that funnel like you live, she said she went back home, and they were kind of stuck on one part of their business, and she said, okay, well I heard Russell say that if I was hired for consulting, I need to be my hook, my story, my offer, I'm gonna go figure things out, so she looked at her pages and tweaked her hooks, tweaked her stories, tweaked her offers, and went through that entire funnel, and watched their company dramatically increase just by applying this one thing, so that's why this is so vitally important for you guys to understand, hooks, story, offer, okay, so even though we go hook, story, offer, I'm gonna start explaining the offer part first, then we're gonna move the story, and then we'll end with hooks, okay, so I'm gonna kind of go backwards on this because that's just the best way to teach it, okay, all right, so we're gonna start with the offer, so the offer, this is really understanding how to create a really good offer is what makes a funnel work, okay, a lot of people know about products, I'm gonna create a product, I'm gonna create this really cool thing, you can click on it, advanced slides, this is the product, right, an offer is different, what is an offer, an offer is bundling together a whole bunch of things to do a couple things, number one is increase the perceived value of it, okay, number two is make it unique, so it's not a commodity, so something specific to you, it's something that's different, that's your own, and it's just one of the most powerful things you can do, okay, so you probably, if you've been following me for any amount of time, you notice I make a lot of offers, my offers usually look like this, I always have an offer slide attached, it says, okay, when you buy this thing from me, you're gonna get the clicker, but you're also gonna get this, and this, and this, and this, and the total value of this entire offer is blank, and I'm gonna just count it to this, okay, that's what an offer slide is, okay, so the offer is the core fundamental part of every single funnel, okay, and again, there's an offer on, why should someone click on your ad? Well, if you click on my ad, I'm gonna give you this, I'm gonna give you a free PDF, I'm gonna give you a video, there's an offer on your landing page, like if you give me your email address, I'm gonna give you this thing, okay, there's an offer on your sales page, if you give me money, I'm gonna give you this thing, like they're all offers, okay, so I wanna put it in perspective how valuable, how important an offer is, okay, this is Claude Hopkins, he is known by many of people in the industry as the father of modern advertising, and he lived in the early 1900s, and his title, he was what they called a ski men, okay, and I think it should become like a cool thing, we should call our funnel builders all ski men, but it's kind of a negative connotation nowadays, but in the early 1900s they called them ski men, and the ski men were responsible for coming with the most important piece of the advertising puzzle, and that was figuring out the offer, okay, so ski men were the offer, now I wanna put this in perspective, in 1907 when Claude Hopkins was doing this, they were paying him $52,000 a year, and he'd be like, oh, that's pretty good money, 15 grand a year's not too bad to sit there and make up offers, but 1907 if you did the calculations on what that actually is worth today, that is $1.396 million in today's dollars, so he's paid almost $1.4 million a year to figure out offers for companies, that's it, that was his role, let me figure out the offer you're gonna sell, okay, that's how valuable a piece this is, and most of us are gonna even think about it, we're like, oh, I'm gonna sell a course, oh, I'm gonna sell a coaching pro, I'm a life coach, I'm gonna sell that, oh, I found these things on Alibaba, I'm gonna sell that, we sell the product, you're missing it, the product's not the thing, if you wanna compete against people selling products, you create offers, okay, the offer will increase the perceived value whatever it is you're selling, and it makes you unique, okay, there's a difference between a product and a commodity, okay, excuse me, a product and an offer, a product is a commodity, right, if I find something at Alibaba, and I wanna sell it, okay, there's 10 or 1000, 100,000 people selling that thing, okay, it's a commodity, so when you have something that's a commodity, the problem is, the only way to win with a commodity is you gotta become the cheapest person, right, so somebody is selling iPhones for 500 bucks on the website, and I'm selling an iPhone, we both have an iPhone, the only way for me to beat them is to sell for a little bit less, so he sells 500, also mine for $400, okay, and then the next person comes along and they go, the only way to beat that is to go, so it's cheaper, so I'm gonna go for 300, and then 200, and the only way to win in your commodity is price, okay, which price is like the stupidest thing to compete on, it's the worst, okay, my very first mentor, Dan Kennedy, told me, said, if you can't be the lowest price leader in town, there's no strategic advantage of being the second lowest price leader, because if you can't be Walmart, be the cheapest, you might as well be the most expensive, because there is a strategic advantage of being the most expensive, okay, so for a lot of us, we're like, okay, I gotta be cheaper than the last person, gotta be cheaper, and we're trying to figure out ways to make ourselves cheaper, okay, instead of doing that, instead of doing that, there's another way to make yourself feel cheaper, okay, you come back here, there's two ways, number one is to decrease the price, that's one way to make you have the cheapest product in town, but the other way is to increase the value, okay, when you increase the value, it makes you feel like the cheapest, right, so if I'm selling a phone, I'm like, hey, if you buy a phone, if it's a product, here's my phone, 500 bucks, this guy sells it at 400, 300, 200, that's one way to become the cheapest product, the second way is, here's the phone at 500 bucks, if you buy it for me, this phone is gonna cost $2,000, but you get three private 101 consults with me through your phone, oh my gosh, that seems cheap, because usually you charge $5,000 an hour for consulting, that seems really cheap now, but I'm selling it for four times as much as this phone, yeah, but it seems cheap now because, why, because the perceived value's increased because I turned it into an actual offer, does that make sense, so that's what we have to start understanding, the worst business to be in is one where you're trying to compete on price, the best business is one where you're increasing the value of the offer and it becomes better and better and more and more sexy, okay, so that's what I'm gonna do is figure out how to create an offer that increases the perceived value of the thing that we're trying to sell, okay, and this is one of the core secrets of funnel buildings, understanding offer, understanding that we've gotta increase the value of this offer, because that is the key, okay, so the offer increases the value, so I'm gonna give you guys a couple examples of this in the real world, hopefully it'll make more sense, okay, so for example, I've been now married for almost 18 years, so I haven't dated in a long, I mean I date my wife, but it's hard for me to remember exactly how it works, but we watch cheesy shows like The Bachelor and Bachelorette, I see my friends dating, my kids are like three or four years away from dating and it makes me really, really nervous, but as I was thinking about this, I started thinking about how did I get my wife to wanna marry me, how did I get her to wanna go on a date with me back in the day, okay, I wanna put this in perspective, my wife is five and a half years older than me, she's way better looking than me, like she has all the things, and I'm like this little dorky kid, okay, when I moved to Boise, Idaho, I was, to put it in perspective, I moved this little apartment with a whole bunch of other wrestlers, I had a shaved head, I had glasses on, I had no future, I was just, I just wanted to wrestle, I was kinda weird, I was socially in there, talked to girls, it was like all these things, right, and all my other roommates were there and a couple of them all had a crush on this girl and she was the older girl and one of the other apartments was close to us and so we'd go over to her house and hang out, right, and so I looked at it and I was watching these guys and they'd come over and they'd try to be like, how cool are they, hey, do you wanna go on a date, hey, do you wanna go on a date, and I watched her, she turned tons of like, bunch of them like, no, I'm not gonna go on a date, no, I'm not gonna go on a date, no, I'm not gonna go on a date, my manager keeps telling these people, no, the offer's not very good, right, everyone's offering like, do you wanna go on a date with me, and when you do that, all they have to look is yourself, like, do I wanna go on a date with you, like, yes or no, right, and some people are like stunning good looking, so your offer increases because of the value of your looks, but I wasn't that way, right, but again, I was a shaved head wrestler with glasses on, and so that was my thing, my value wasn't huge on looks, and so I was coming there, I'm like, how do I increase the value of this package? Like, okay, what do I got? So when I asked her on a date, it wasn't just like, hey, do you wanna go on a date with me? She's like, ah, other guys are way better looking than you, I said, okay, I gotta make this an offer, okay, so I was like, okay, if you wanna date with me, first thing we're gonna do is we're gonna do this, and then we're gonna go on this, and we're gonna experience this, and we're gonna eat here and do this, and then afterwards we're gonna go do this thing right here, we're gonna meet these people here, and I create an experience, right, and then I asked her on a date, and she's like, oh my gosh, that offer, that experience, that whole thing sounds amazing, and you're in there, and that sounds great too, like let's go and do this thing, right, but I create an offer, I asked her on a date, she said yes, and now we're married, five kids later, and it's amazing, okay, so if you're struggling in dating, this is my only dating advice I will ever give anybody, is how do you increase your offer? Okay, if it's just you, you may be in trouble, especially if you're not very good looking, right, so I get to figure out, how do I increase the offer of this, okay, what else do I have to offer my future spouse, this person on a date, right, so there's my dating advice, okay, but you see how it works, I mean increasing the value of yourself as the offer, okay, as I was teaching, we did an event earlier this year called, I'm gonna lock the secrets event for our two Comic Club coaching members, and it was kind of a fun event, and I decided to invite all of the kids to come to it as well, so the parents came and the kids came as well, and so I'm explaining this concept of increasing an offer to the audience, and I think all the parents were just like, wait a minute, I got a question, wait a minute, and the parents are always a little bit slower than the kids, kids pick up stuff really fast, and I was sitting out in the audience, I was looking out, and I started asking the kids, I could tell that the parents were a little perplexed, I said okay, I wanna get some kids out, so I called on some kids, and this right here on the left end of this image, this is Ryan Lee's remainder circle members, and on the right hand here is his daughter, Keanna, and Keanna, I don't know how, she's probably 10, maybe 12 years old, and she runs a babysitting business, and so I asked her, I said, how would you ask all the kids, for all the kids, how would you increase the offer of whatever businesses you're working on? And she raises her hand, so I throw her the little catch box, and she grabs it, she says, well I'm a babysitter, and I figure all my friends are also babysitters, we're all charging five bucks an hour, what if I increase my offer by doing this? And she's like, what if I told them that after we, I'm gonna feed your kids healthy food, not like pizza? And then after this thing's done, I'm gonna actually wash the dishes, and I'm gonna make sure the kids are bed at nine o'clock, and I'm gonna play games with the kids when we go out, and then I'm gonna do, and she needed about four or five different things that she could do, and I looked at her and I was like, oh my gosh, do you wanna watch my kids, my kids were at the event, I'm like, can you watch them tonight? That offer was amazing. You look at like, there's 10 babysitters, and all of them are like, oh baby, sure kid, oh baby, sure kid, oh baby, sure kid, oh baby, sure kid, and when I do that, I'm also gonna do this, and this, and this, and this, and like, who are you gonna give your money to? She could double, triple quadruple her prices, and she still seems the cheapest, because not only did she watch my kids and make sure they didn't die, she also cleaned up the house, fed them healthy food, had activities with them, played with them, wasn't texting on her phone all night long, all those kind of things as well. She CPA certified all those things, like she created an offer where she could double, triple quadruple her price, and she still would have seemed the cheapest. She would have a line of people out the door waiting for them to babysit, because she created an actual offer instead of just selling a product. Okay, do you guys see how this works? So you're sitting there in your marketplace, and you're like, I've got this course, I've got this physical product, I've got this training program, this coaching program, this event, like whatever the thing is that you're trying to do, you just step back and figure out, okay, I'm competing with all these other people, how do I make my offer better? What do I have that can be better than what everybody else is doing? I gotta one up everybody else, I gotta make my offer better, okay, so that the perceived value of it seems so much higher, that way it seems cheaper, and then I don't have to lower my price, in fact, I can increase my price, and it won't even matter, okay? It's called price elasticity. So if you look at any offer I create, we spend so much time, like what we literally do is I'll come out like, this is an example, this is the one funnel way offer. When we create this offer, it was literally me and my team in front of the whiteboard, like hey, let's make an amazing offer, like what should we give people? And we had a big whiteboard, it said like potential products on the top, we started listing, well, what if we give them this, what if we give them this, oh, remember that training you did over here, we can give them that, and like, oh, what if we give them this worksheet here, what if we hadn't fly to your house, and you could just build their funnel form, what if we did this, what if we gave them a click funnel, so we just mapped out as many different things as possible, so we had a whole whiteboard, all the potential things that we could possibly could do when we sold one funnel away, right? But we didn't give them everything, but we brainstormed like that, and that got all these amazing ideas out, and then we came back to our stack slide, and we said okay, what should we actually give them, we said okay, well the first thing we're gonna give them, we're gonna give them 30 days of video missions from Russell, what's the value of that? And 30 days of coaching from Steven and from Julie, and then we went from a customized 30 day kit, and then we send them a physical book in the mail, and then a physical MP3 player, then this hardcover book, and then access the interviews, and we start building out the thing, and also this is an insane offer, that most other competitors would sell us for at least a thousand bucks or more, and they're like, let's sell it for this, right? Okay, and that's how we created an amazing offer. Okay, and we do this every single time. If you ever notice anything you buy from me, there's always gonna be a stack slide like this. There never will not be a stack slide like this, okay? The first time I do an offer without a stack slide, you guys should just fire me, because it means I failed at my job. I'm a horrible ski man, I'm not worth the $1.3 million a year that I charge to be a ski man, okay? Like this is the key, you gotta figure out what is the offer, okay? And if something's not selling, again I told you, it's always either the hook, the story of the offer, so if someone's not working, look at that, like maybe my offer sucks, maybe nobody wants this, what else could we include instead? What if I got rid of this and plug this in instead? What if I got rid of this and plug this in instead? What if I did this and this, and all of a sudden it opens up your mind to like, oh my offer just sucked. I get the right offer, people are gonna go crazy for it, because it seems so cheap, right? Okay, so that's the game, that's the game with creating an offer. So what you do, your job is to sit down with your team, with yourself, with your spouse, with your kids, whatever it is, okay, what potential products can I do here? Okay, when Keanu was doing it for a babysitting program, product, she's like, okay, well I could wash the dishes, I could clean the bathroom, I could cook healthy food, I could play games with the kids, and she started making all these things, and that gave her the power of the things to be able to create. Okay, we were at the kid event, at the unlock the secrets event, I brought these kids on stage too, and we said, okay, you're a lawn mowing company, okay, everyone out there is mowing lawns, what could you do to increase the value? And these kids were like, what if we scrubbed their windows? What if we did this, and these kids, all the time they were like, make pointy ideas out, and building insane offers, and I said, man, everyone else is charging 30 bucks to mow lawn, I'd pay you 500 bucks to mow lawn, because you're also doing boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, right, you increase the value of the offer, okay, and we gave them all these things, the lawn mowing, we did, what if we did this kind of business, and this kind, we started giving them the throwing out of these random businesses, and watched these kids start getting really good at increasing the value of the offer. Okay, so for you, you gotta become a master of that too, just like these kids, what else could you offer, what else could you offer, what else could you offer, keep building this potential product up, okay, and that's where you're gonna have this pallet of offers. Now this is important, as we get to funnel building tomorrow, you need to have tons of potential products, okay, because if you're doing a cart funnel, you need a product on your first page, you need products for your upsells, your downsells, your order form bumps, so this is something you're continually adding, continually figuring out, we have a whole drive full of trainings, and products, and software, and worksheets, and the list, everything we ever create in the past all goes somewhere that can become a potential product in the future. Okay, now I'm filming this funnelology course, this will be a course we sell on its own, but it'll probably be a bonus somewhere else, it'll probably be somewhere like, I'm always building up my inventory of potential products, and then when I have an idea, okay, I create the product, I look through here, what are the things, can I bundle with it to make it amazing, and boom, now I've got a sexy offer, okay? So for you guys, this should be a thing that you're doing, not just once, but like, every week, sit down with your team, sit down with yourself, figure out what else could I offer, what else could I offer? Okay, because until you get the right offer, it gets really hard to get people to give you money. You get the right offer, it's really easy, okay? All right, so that's your homework, is go down, we list out your potential products, and then from that, figure out which things I'm actually, am I willing to go and do, okay? So again, like when the one funnel way challenge, like one of the ideas was like, fly to Russell's house, and then build your funnel, like, okay, that sounds amazing, but for 100 bucks, I'm not gonna do that. So I don't have to put it on the offer slide, I just put things on the offer side that I'm willing to do, okay, I figure out what are my non-negotials, I'm not gonna do this, not gonna do this, not gonna do this, but here are the things I am willing to do, I figure this thing out, I put it on my offer side, and boom, now I've got a sexy offer, now I've got something to go and test, okay? All right, so there's offer. Now this is important to understand is that, you're gonna be using offers again on every single page of your funnel, okay? So you look at this right here, there's gonna be offers on your ads, okay? I'm making, like every time someone clicks on my ad, I'm making an offer, click on this ad, I'm gonna show you a free video, click on this ad, I'm gonna give you a template, I'm gonna give you a checklist, click on this ad to go, you know what I'm saying? You're right, so every single, like every single ad, there's an offer, they go to my landing page, like give me your email address, I'm gonna give you this, there's an offer there, okay? Give me money, I'm gonna give you this, there's an offer there, if I buy my app, so I'm gonna give you this, there's an offer there, okay? So it's important to start building on this inventory because you're using these offers over and over and over and over again in different places, okay? Some people wonder why I'm able to get products up and launched and live so fast, it's because I've been building for the last decade and a half an inventory of products that I can go and start plugging into things, okay? Is your time to start building those things up? Okay, every time you record something, every time you put a microphone on it, every time you interview somebody, those are potential products you could be selling, okay? So start capturing and start cataloging and start having them ready for yourself so that when you're ready to create your own offers, you've got these things you can start plugging into places, okay? All right, so there is the offer section, okay? So come back here, we got hook, we got story, we got offer, okay? So the offer is the key. So I'm gonna go back one step because the offer increases the value, but the next thing that increases the value, you tell a story about the offer that also increases the value of it, okay? So we're gonna start now with stories. Okay, now here's why stories are essential. If I'm working with somebody, my dream client, and I wanna really serve them, okay? I found my dream client, okay? For someone to change their destiny, they have to make a decision. Okay, if I wanna get this person over here so I can change their life, they've gotta make a decision, okay? For them to make a decision, they have to change their state. Most people are in a state where they're not happy, they're miserable, and for me to grab them, hook them, and give them to go find their wallet and give me money excitedly to go on a journey with me to change their life, like I've gotta change their state, okay? And the best way to give them, make a decision, change their state, and the best way to change their state is by telling them a story, okay? So hook them, I tell them a story, the story changes their state, the state helps them make a decision, and the decision changes their destiny. See how those things all fall together? So if I don't tell them a story, if I just throw it in the, hey, you should buy my iPhone, don't want my iPhone, no, I'm out, boom, okay? But if I hook them, okay, and then boom, I change their state, I tell them a story to change their state, to give them something to make a decision so they can change their destiny, okay? So an offer without a state change is useless, that's what you gotta understand. An offer without a state change is useless. Okay, so the big secret, you have to become really good at story selling. Now a lot of you guys have seen me map this out before. This is kind of the big premise of the Expert Secrets book, but it's so important. It's understanding this concept that we call the Epiphany Bridge, okay? Now a lot of you guys have heard me talk about this before, but I wanna map it out for the new people, but also hopefully some of you guys will get a different aha or epiphany as you're listening to it, okay? So if you think about this, I want you to go back in time to when you were a normal human. You guys remember that? Before you were excited about whatever you're doing now. Before I was excited about funnels, before I was excited about wrestling, before I was excited about whatever the thing is that you sell. Okay, back when you were just a normal human and you were just wandering around the universe, like doing whatever you do before you bumped into something, right? So remember that, this is you at the very beginning, okay? Then something happened to you, right? Some experience happened. I want you to start thinking about what was the experience? For me, what was the experience that got me excited about funnels? Excuse me, okay? If I'm selling wrestling, what was the experience that got me excited about wrestling? Something happened, okay? Some experience happened and it changed you, right? And what changed you, you went on this journey, okay? I call this the epiphany bridge because this is like you had this epiphany of like, oh my gosh, I need a funnel. Oh my gosh, like I'm a wrestler. Like I want to be a wrestler. Oh my gosh, I want to be a drummer. Like what was the story? What was the experience that happened that gave you this epiphany, okay? Why don't you remember that? And then realize that after you had that epiphany and you were sold like I want to do, like you had this emotional thing that sold you emotion. Like this is amazing. You went on this journey and then you started like deaking out, right? That's for me, I was like, I learned about funnels and I was like, oh my gosh, this is amazing, right? To me, this is for my epiphany story, right? Like I'm not gonna go deep into it, but selling my potato gun, I sold my potato gun through a funnel and it had success. And I was like, oh, the angels were singing. Like I could use this in any business. And I went and launched like 400 new companies in the next like two days all using funnels because the potato gun funnel made money, right? That was my big epiphany. And so I started geeking out, I'd go on this journey over here and on the other side of this journey is where we geek out and put glasses. This is my, let's give this guy a little hair. There you go. So you geek out and you come on this side of the journey and all of a sudden here, you become very logical. You're like, this is amazing. Do you realize that the reason why funnels are so important because if I increase the average cart value and I get my CPA down and my blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, then I can make a ton of money, right? We started geeking out logically and you start understanding, oh, if you do this and you do this and like you get all the techno, but we call this techno babble, right? Techno babble. And you become this like genius at your thing. Okay, and what's wrong with, the problem with this is that for most of us and you, is you come so good at this thing that you're just like, I am a genius. I can go convert the whole world and you run out and you go find these unexpected little humans who are walking around and you're like, guess what? They're like, what? And you're like, let me tell you about funnels. You start spewing out all this information you have about CPAs and average cart values and upsells and downsells, order for months and everything and they're just like, I don't know. And they have no idea what is happening. They freak out and they run away, right? I see this in every single industry. I see this in network marketing is probably the worst, right? You get sold on something, you come over here, you get all the research, you go to the convention, you go to the event, you learn all the science behind why are things so exciting? And then you go and you find your prospects and you pounce on them and you spew out all your techno babble and you scare the crap out of them. Okay, it happens over and over and over. I see trainers do this, okay? They got big old muscles and they find some guy who's like, oh, I want muscles like yours and they start talking to you about lactic acid, about carbs and protein versus in your macros and you're just like, I just want biceps, right? And that's the problem is we think we're so smart that we spew all this techno babble on, okay? We have to understand if you want to become good at selling, you cannot sell through techno babble. Okay, this is logic. You were not logically convinced to go on this journey. I know, I guarantee you, if you go back in time and start thinking, okay, sometimes it's hard for you to be like, what was the thing back here? What was the emotional experience I had that took me on this journey? We have to remember that is, okay? Because we've spent so much time geeking out that we think this is all this and this is what we try to sell people with and it kills our sales every single time. If you want to sell anything, the secret is to come back on this journey, back to where you were at when you were a normal human and remember that. Because that's where the person that you're about to meet is at. Okay, when they click on your ad, they show up, this is where they're at. They have not come into this journey. They're not gonna geek out with you right now. Okay, but they're here right now and you're coming in, they just clicked on your ad. Your job, your goal, okay, you already hooked them. You got this little window telling the story. You need to tell them a story about why you care about this stuff. Why were you emotionally sold? Why should they care? You tell your story and if you do it in the right way, and again, this is outside of the context of this training, this is the expert seekers book, if you tell them the right way, they will have the same epiphany that you just had and they'll go on their own journey. They'll study it, they'll geek out, they'll figure out all the math and the science and technology behind it. But that's not what sells them. What sells them is this emotional experience. It's you telling them your story that breaks their false beliefs, that gets them to wanna come on this journey. That's the big a-ha, that's the big secret. So you gotta remember this, what was your story? So when you have that window where you hook somebody, you're telling the story. If you start spewing your techno, you will lose them. You tell them your story about how this thing changed your life, you give them the epiphany, then they'll make the state change they'll meet, they'll make the decision they need to make, all things will happen, and you'll be able to actually serve them. That's the big secret. So I'll come back to my slides here. Here's the epiphany bridge, right? There's our person ahead of time, they have the a-ha, they go on the epiphany bridge, they logically geek out, cross out the techno babble. So that's what you gotta understand, this is the key. All right. It's like I should ask for any questions, but we're, there's a live audience, but I'm sitting here. So, any questions? If so, you should ask your neighbor, just kidding. So that's the epiphany bridge. This is like one of the biggest realizations I've had over the last five or six years, and I've seen as people have taken this principle, it's the key, they make sure ads work, and make sure your videos work, your podcasts work, everything is like, understanding it's this story. So you, remembering these stories, you're gonna be better and better at telling stories, that'll increase your conversion, right? I said, if a funnel isn't working, it's always your hook, your story, your offer. Okay, we talked about offer earlier, but if it's not your offer, if that offer's like, this offer's insane, well, your story's wrong. You're not positioning, you're not making them, like if you tell the story the right way, it increases the value of the offer you're about to make them, right? So if they're not buying the offer, means your story wasn't right, you gotta tell the story differently, figure out the story, cut out the technical battle, cut out the confusing words, cut out all the things that are keeping them from success, and figure out, how do I tell the story in a way that gets them excited so they have the same epiphany that I just had? So if you can do that, they're gonna come on the journey themselves, they sell themselves, okay? All right, so I'm gonna go through this. This is the next piece of this, is to understand, is your customers, in fact, I'm gonna draw this as we go through this. So your customers, all right now, when they come to you, they've got, if they understood everything correctly right now, they would already have bought with you, right? So the reason why they haven't came to you yet is they've got these different false beliefs, okay? And these could be from our society, it could be from trial and error, it could be from a million different things, right? But all of them have these false beliefs, okay? So I call these the chains of false beliefs. So here's the person, and they got these chains of false beliefs that are holding back, okay? So that's the first thing that stands, there's these chains of false beliefs, okay? So if you have a thing about, what were the false beliefs you had before you went on your journey, okay? Like for funnels, like I thought the best way to make money was on selling stuff on eBay, or on Craigslist, or I thought, you know, you had a degree to make money, or I thought you had a business license, like, I had always had false beliefs of why I couldn't be successful when I got started. It's hard for me to remember now, because I just think everybody can be successful, but there was a time back when I had these chains of false beliefs that held me back. And the reality is most people who are gonna be clicking on my ads have the same false beliefs right now. That's number one, right? They gotta think about why they have this false belief. Well, something happened to them in their life, right? Something happened, I'm gonna be a black guy, he's got beat up, right? There's some experience that happened, they gave them this false belief, right? And they remember this, it's like inside their head, like this is the experience that happened, they gave me this false belief. Okay, here's a front of your face, man. There's this experience, right? Some more ones that change false beliefs. Number two is the experience that they had. So when you start thinking for yourself, like why did you believe that thing? What was the reason why you believed it? Well, I believed it was hard to make money because my parents, and this isn't true for me, but my parents struggle with money, and so because they said it was hard to make money, I believe it was hard to make money, right? Or whatever it might be, there's some experience that happened, they keeps them, they gave them this false belief, right? So because of this experience, they have a story in their mind, they're like, well, this is, this is, here's a story, okay, they have a story, like well, this is the story because, like I can't be successful here because of whatever story, right? So that's, and I'm gonna show you guys an example this year in a second, so it'll make more sense as we kind of go through here. So they've got this story about why, why, like why this thing is this false belief, and so your job then is to come through and figure out how to tell them a better story. Does that make sense? If not, that's okay, cause I'm about to show it to you. So let me give you guys an example of this, okay? So I'm gonna tease my network marketing friends, I love them, I've been involved in multiple network marketing companies, I own ownership in a couple of them as well, so I love network marketers, but I'm still gonna tease you a little bit because it's just kind of fun. So here we go, so pretend like this is me, this is Devon from Liking Live, Devon comes up to me and he's like, hey Russell, I want you to join my company, I'm like, sweet dude, I'm in, this sounds awesome, I sign up, and he's like, all right, see those guys right there, Pam and Jim? I need you to go sign them up. Like, okay, here we go. So I'm gonna go over there and talk to Pam and Jim. Now, first thing I think about is, okay, what's the, what's the chain of false belief that Jim already has, okay? He doesn't believe network marketing is the right vehicle to make money for some reason, right? Okay, so I think he believes it's not right. Why does he believe that? What's the experience he has? So think about that, okay, maybe it's because Michael Scott tackled him and tried to pitch him on something, so he's got, this is the experience, like I had this bad experience, that's why I believe this. So now he's got a story in his head, like what's the story he tells himself? The story tells himself, I don't know if he has this episode or not, if not it's worth Googling and or getting on Hulu or whatever and watching, is that all network marketing things are pyramid schemes, right? So this is the false belief that runs Jim's life, okay? Now based on his false belief, I can pitch him any opportunity on planet earth, no matter how good, no matter how legitimate it is, and he is gonna say, no, no matter what, because he has this false belief because this experience, he's built a story in his mind, okay? So it doesn't matter how many times he clicks on my ad, how many times I pitch him, how good my presentation is, how good my, like how sexy my, the cars I'm driving, like, no matter what I do, no matter, it's never gonna work because his false belief is so ingrained in his mind because of these life experiences that have happened, okay? The only way that I can beat him, the only way that I can not beat him, the only way I can get him to believe something different is to tell him a better story, okay? That's when we come in and we tell our Pifty Bridge story, okay? We tell a story that rewrites his story. I was in a private mastermind group in Puerto Rico about six months ago and I was sitting there with a whole bunch of just amazing marketers and one of the guys named Craig Clemens, who's probably one of the greatest living copywriters on the planet earth right now. And he was talking about writing copy in the stories and he's something fascinating to me. He said, my job as a marketer is to rewrite people's stories. I was like, oh my gosh, that's what he's talking about here, right? So if I come here and Jim's like, I think that network marketing is a scam, the reason is because my friends knocked me over and because I believe it's pyramid scheme. Well, if I come back and say, well, that's awesome. In fact, Jim, I believe the exact same thing. I used to be just, like I used to be identical to you. Like I believe the same thing and I never believed it until one day I saw this guy on the internet and he was generating leads online. It was the most weird thing. And he wasn't calling his friends, he wasn't calling his family members. He was buying Facebook and Google ads. They're coming to a forum, they filled out and he called those people. Because they asked him to call them, they actually answered the phone and he's getting like one out of three people he called to actually sign up. It was the craziest thing in the world. And so I was like, well, if I don't talk to my friends and my family, I'm willing to at least try it. So I went and I tried it and went through this process and this is a picture of me winning a Ferrari. I was the number one money earner in a company. I won a Ferrari and I didn't actually talk to a single human being ever. I did it all through the internet. So I tell him that story and I'll send Jim's like, oh my gosh, if my story trumps his, then he changes his belief pattern. That's the key. That's how this whole game is played. So it's coming back and really understanding this, saying, okay, what are the reasons, sorry about that, what are the reasons why they're not gonna believe me? What's the experience they went through, they caused them that, and what's the story they're telling themselves, and then what story do I have that'll trump that? What story do I have that'll rewrite the story inside of his head? All of a sudden Jim's like, oh my gosh, that's amazing. If I could actually, because I always wanted to make money in a network marketing, but I didn't wanna get beat up, I didn't want to talk to my friends. If I could do this through this other avenue, that would change everything. That's the magic. So the next question people always ask me is like, oh, that's cool, but what stories do I actually tell? And so there's a lot of stories, and I'm gonna walk you through this because we call this the big domino. The big domino is a concept actually was at an event in New York probably six or seven years ago now, before ClickFunnels came out. And at this event, Tim Ferris won the keynote speakers, and Tim got on stage and was interesting, because everyone's asking questions and stuff, and then one guy raised his hand and said, I have a question, what question should it be asking you that nobody's asked you yet? Tim kinda smiled for a second, he said, you know what, people always ask me, what's my day look like? Can you follow me around with the camera and see what my life is like? He's like, I always tell people no, he's like the reason, he's like if you saw my life, it's actually really, really boring. He said, I wake up in the morning and I drink some tea, and I sit on my couch and I look out the window, I think for a couple hours, and I go and I'll read a book or two, then I'll listen to the podcast, and I'll walk around and go on a walk, and then I'll think some more. He's like I might do that for a week or a month or a couple months sometimes, he's like, because I'm not doing a lot, he's like what I'm doing is they most people, they spread out their day where they're like, okay, here's my two lists, there's a thousand things they're doing, I think after thinking, I bust out all their stuff, he said, for me it's different, he said, I try to sit there, and I'll spend a month or two months or three months, try to figure out what is the one big domino, that if I knock down that domino, either knocks down all the other dominoes, or makes them irrelevant. And I was like, oh my gosh, that's powerful. Like what is the one big domino? And I started realizing this in sales, there's always a big domino in every single sales presentation, like what's the one thing, if I get you to believe one thing, then you're gonna come and you're gonna, you're gonna come to me, right? You're gonna start using funnels, you're gonna start losing weight through my weight loss program, or you're gonna, whatever it is that you sell, right? There's one big domino that if you can knock that down, makes all the other dominoes irrelevant or obsolete. And so that's the big thing, is we try to tell stories in a way that's gonna knock down the big domino so we can get them. Now inside of our marketing, there's basically this four core stories we tell, and we'll walk through this. So the very first story in any presentation is called the origin story, okay? So the origin story, come back here, this is typically the origin story, the origin story about why you do what you do, why you're so excited, okay? Now if you're doing this in like a perfect webinar, like you did the perfect webinar presentation, most of you guys had a chance to go with that training, hopefully in the past, if you haven't, the perfect webinar training is a 90 minute sales presentation we do through webinars, through Facebook Live, through on stage, things like that. This is kind of, the first 15 minutes of your presentation is the origin story, okay? This is a 90 minute presentation. So if you only got 10 minutes or if you got a 15 minute presentation, it shrinks, right? But it's basically one sixth of your presentation is your origin story, okay? So this is you telling the story about why you believe or you believe. So if you see my final lecture presentation, this was my origin story, okay? The first one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, 10, 11, 12, 13. My first 13 slides right there are me telling my origin story about why I'm so excited about funnels, okay? So I tell that first origin story and for some people, when I tell that story, they're like, oh my gosh, I need to funnel my business too, right? Some of them like, they got the a-ha and they're ready to go buy. They're ready to go run and just give me money because they're sold, right? So my very first, my origin story is the very first chance I have to push this big domino down. And again, for a percentage of your audience, you tell that one story and that will push the domino down and boom, they are in, they are ready to do whatever as you're doing, right? They're following on this path, they're geeking out now, they're studying, they're learning and they will be a faithful follower for the rest of your life, okay? Now, unfortunately, not everybody believes as easy as that, right? So what happens is if the first domino is knocked down, if the origin story is knocked down, what happens is you use just three things to keep it up, okay? The three dominoes that get it stuck. There's the vehicle, the internal belief and the external belief, okay? So these are the next three stories, okay? So if the domino doesn't fall, I gotta tell three other sub-stories. One of them is about the vehicle. So the vehicle is the thing you're trying to put them into, right? So my vehicle is funnels. I'm gonna get you guys to believe that funnels are the greatest thing, it's the best way to grow your company. Your vehicle could be something different. It's like, this is my weight loss process is the best. My process for saving relationships is the best. My process for whatever is the vehicle you're trying to put your potential customers in, right? That is, that's the first false belief, okay? Second one is the intro, because their ability to actually have success with your vehicle, right? So somebody may say, well, Russell, this is awesome. I believe that funnels will change my life, but I just don't know if I can do it. I'm not technical, I don't know how to do things like that, right? So the internal beliefs about themselves. And then third false belief that keeps you back from success is maybe it's maybe like, I believe in funnels, I believe it could work for me, but there's this external thing, I don't know how to drive traffic, okay? Let's say it's a weight loss plan, like I believe that Keto's the best thing in the world. I believe I could actually do it, but my wife, she buys sugar and ice cream and there's gonna be in her house, I couldn't do it. There's the external thing, something else that keeps me from being successful, right? Okay, so that's the next step here. So as I moved to story number two, this is my story about the vehicle.
