So I'm gonna tell them the Pifty Bridge story about why I believe this vehicle is the best vehicle in the world. That's happening in the second 15 minutes of my presentation, or the second 1-6th of my presentation. So in here, I come back here, I say, what's the false belief pattern that somebody has? What's the chain of false belief? Why do they experience that? What's the story they're telling themselves and what story can I use to trumpet? So for me, if I'm telling you funnels are the greatest thing in the world, and you're like, I don't know if it really is, then here in my presentation, I do a presentation where I talk about the depth of a website. So here are my slides, this is where I give this part of the presentation. I talk about secret number one, I go through, I tell the story about funnel hacking and Tony Robbins, and I go through this, how I found a funnel that worked, and I funnel hacked it, and I modeled it, and I go through the whole thing, and these slides right here, now tell my Pifty Bridge story about the vehicle. Takes me about 15 minutes to go through that part of the story, if I do it correctly, then if my story trumps theirs, then boom, the domino falls, and they're ready to sign up. So I've had two chances so far to knock down this domino, and every time I do this, another percentage of people are like, boom, I'm in, boom, I'm in, boom, I'm in, okay? Then I move to story number three, which goes back to the internal beliefs, okay? So if they're still like, okay, that's cool, Russell, I believe funnel is the greatest thing in the world, I don't believe I can do it, then I'm gonna come back and say, okay, well, what are the chains of false beliefs about why they don't think they can do it? What was the experience they probably had? So for me, it might be like, well, that's cool, but I'm not technical, so I don't think I can do it. Okay, well, why did they experience that? Well, I tried to build something one time with WordPress, and it just, it didn't work, and it was frustrating. Okay, so what's the story of telling them? I'm not a technical person, I can't build funnels. So I got a trumpet saying, okay, well, look, this is so simple, I made, we even had Grant Cardone, the least technical person on planet Earth who actually built an entire funnel in a plane, let me show you how it worked, right? And then boom, I go into my story, story number three, I go through, I go through all my slides, where I tell the third epiphany bridge story, okay? It's my third pass, and if my story trumps theirs, then boom, the domino falls, and they come in, and they're ready to get signed up, okay? And then the fourth story then is the external belief, okay? So they believe in funnels, they believe they could actually, I can be, I watch how easy it is to click funnels, I can actually use, I can actually build funnels now, but I don't know how to drive traffic, so I'm out. Okay, I got one more story, okay? My last story is my external belief story. This takes the last 15 minutes of the hour, okay? So I come here, what's the false belief, why do they believe they can't do it? Well, they tried to drive traffic in the past, and it didn't work, what's the experience? Or sorry, the chain of false belief is like, why don't we believe that I, you know, I don't know how to get traffic, traffic is really hard, okay? It's really expensive, why do they believe that? Well, I tried to buy ads on LinkedIn one time, it was like 45 bucks a lead, so it was really expensive, so I stopped trying. Okay, so what's the story, I tell them themselves stories, like well, traffic's expensive and hard. Okay, well actually, let me teach this, here's this really cool way that I get traffic, and it doesn't cost me hardly anything. And then I go through the Pifty Bridge story, tell the whole Pifty Bridge story about that, I show them the whole process, if my story trumps theirs, they're like, oh my gosh, I actually could drive traffic, this is amazing, I believe in funnels, I believe I can get traffic, okay? And if I've done it right, then that knocks it down, all the dollars are down, and they're just waiting to come and give me money, okay? So that's the power, and then the last step then is to make the offer. And so here I go through, I make my offer, go into the stack slide, that's the last 30 minutes of my presentation, so a 90 minute presentation, or if it's again a 30 minute presentation, it's the last two-sixth, or one-third, that right, one-third of my presentation, okay? And I go through there, and that's kinda how that part works there, okay? So if you've watched any of my presentations, you guys have seen this, this is the process, how it works. Origin story, story about the vehicle, story about the internal beliefs, story about the external beliefs, stack in the clothes, boom, $100 million a year company. Okay, that's the whole thing, that's what we did, okay? That's the framework, how it works. Okay. All right, I got a couple more quick things here, and then we're gonna break, does that sound good? I know that as I tell you guys this process of telling stories, and being emotional, and all these kinda things that can be kinda hard or scary, so those days we were just getting started, and I wanted to show you some videos of me when I got started, because I guarantee you, you are better than I am right now. Who wants a piece of that? So that's the joke. So if you're at Funaliking Live this year, feel free to make fun of me. Who wants a piece of that? So anyway, this is, hopefully give you guys some encouragement, like the more you do this, the better you'll get at actually doing it. You just gotta do it and practicing, getting it out there. I want you to understand, like, the more simple your product is, the easier you can do this, right? So if I have a really simple product, like hey, don't have it on my iPhone, I can tell you one story and you're like sweet, I'm in. So you just have one Piphany Bridge story, make them an offer and that's it. If I'm trying to get you to click on an ad, my story doesn't have to be long. Just like when you grab a hook, tell you a quick story, you can click on my ad right now, and they click on the ad, like the story can be short and simple. If I'm selling a complex product, like a $1,000 sale or a $5,000 sale or whatever, that's when you go through and you gotta have story for your origin story, your vehicle, your internal belief, your external belief. So the more expensive, the more complicated your product is, the more stories you gotta tell, the more simple the less you can do. So that's kind of an important part to understand as well. So we talked earlier about you guys printing together like a product inventory. The next thing on your homework time is to go start printing together a story inventory. You come back here and say, okay, what are the chains of false beliefs my customers have? Okay, I have a false belief about this and about this and about this and about this, okay? And let's tell at least 10 false beliefs that your customers have. Step number one, okay? And if you can't think of false beliefs, like I don't know my customers' false beliefs, so I got two options. Number one is think back about yourself and think back when you were here, before you started this, when you were back when you were a normal person, what were the false beliefs you had or you would have had if somebody came and tried to pitch you on this? Okay, think about it, what are the false beliefs? Put those things in there. Okay, and the second thing is if you don't know, then go ask people. Go to somebody, okay, I think you should build a funnel and they're like, why? And you explain it and start listing, what are the false beliefs that you keep coming back with? You talk to it through people, you're gonna find it real quick, like these are all the false beliefs people have. Okay, so figure out what are the things that are the chains of false beliefs that are holding back? Then the next line say, what's the experience that they probably experienced that made them have that chain of false belief? What was the actual experience? Think about that and write it down, okay? Then now I said, okay, now this is the experience they had, what's the story that they're telling themselves? Okay, the story is this, they have a story that's happening in their head, subconsciously, it's running their head every single time that someone comes in with an offer, an opportunity like this, they got a story, it's not gonna work because of this, it's not gonna work because of this, they always have the story running in their head. So your job is to figure out what that story is because you have to trump their story. You gotta tell a better story to overwrite their story, okay? So you're listening to the story and then what's the epiphany bridge, what story do you have that's better that's gonna rewrite the story in their mind so they can actually move forward with you, okay? So for you guys, you need to start building out your inventory of stories, okay? You should have 10, you should have 20, you should have 100, how many stories do you guys hear me tell? This is my podcast, watch my Facebook live, this training alone, how many stories have I probably told? There's a lot, right? You gotta start building out your inventory of stories. The reason why I tell you guys stories is that I know that as I'm telling you guys different pieces of this, everybody has to have the false concerns because of the false beliefs, oh, that's not gonna work because of this, or because of this, or because of this. So I'm inserting stories in my teaching to try to destroy those false beliefs so you can continue with me on this journey, okay? Because if I tell you something 15 minutes into this training and you get a false belief, you're not gonna believe anything else to say afterwards. So I tell a story to crush that so you can continue to believe and you keep coming with me on this journey, okay? All right, so that's the story part. So we talked about we got hook, story, and offer, okay? So the last piece in this bait is the hook, okay? We talked about the offer, okay? The offer increases the value, we talked about the story, the story increases the value of the offer, and then the hook, the hook actually does not provide any value, okay? All the hook does is it grabs their attention just long enough that you can tell them the story and then make them the offer, okay? So every story you create has multiple different hooks, okay? The hooks could be, I mean, there's tons of them. It could be the picture of the thing, it could be a story, it could be a goofy picture, it could be a video, it could be an image, it could be hooks are the things that's grabbing attention. Okay, so think about your dream customers sitting on their phones, strolling through things, or we give them the stop scrolling. That's what hook is, okay? So where do you find hooks? If you go online, every single ad you see is a hook. Okay, every one of my ads is a hook, every ad from every advertiser you have are hooks. So I'm looking, you'll start seeing the hooks, okay, they're out there, and start noticing where the little ones make you stop, and they're like, hey, what's that? Oh, what's that? Okay, I literally, every single day, I spend a few minutes every single, excuse me, a couple times a day, scrolling through Facebook, I scroll through Instagram and YouTube, and all I'm doing is I'm looking for things that grab my attention. So let's say I stop it, I take a picture, like that grabbed my attention, I don't know why, but let me see, was it the headline, was it the image, was it the word, was it the video, was it something goofy they did, was it the way they presented something, what was the hook that got me to stop in my tracks? I look at that, I start studying them, I start getting ideas for hooks I can use in my marketing. So there's tons of them, okay, so I recommend you start going, look at the two common common pointers, people who've already been successful, look at theirs and say, what are their hooks that are throwing out there? What are the ads they have out there? There's tons of good ones, this is Drew Manning, fit to fat to fit. His hooks, if you watch this, this is one of my favorite hooks, it's him on Jay Leno, he was a fit trader who gained a whole bunch of weight and got fat, and lost much weight and got fit. So one of his hooks is him on Jay Leno, there's hooks of him before and after, and before after, wait, before after, after pictures, as hooks, there's pictures of the students, there's pictures of clients, there's pictures of steak, there's pictures of cake, there's pictures, like all these things are hooks you throw out there to grab people and pull them into his keto funnels. Okay, Natalie Hodson has a product called Abs Core Pelvic Floor, it helps women to be able to strengthen their, so that they don't do that, I don't even know how to explain it. Anyway, the ad of her peeing her pants during a live video is what got people to, is a hook that grabbed people's attention and brought them into her funnels. She launched this funnel with this hook and did a million dollars in the first four months. Because that hook grabbed her dream customers who had this problem and sucked them into her funnels, it was amazing, right? The right hook is gonna grab so many people and bring into her funnels. Garrett White, this is one of his hooks. Learn to unlock nearly unlimited sex power and money without having to cheat on your wife, get a divorce, ignore your children, leave your church to date with drugs or party like a rock star in Las Vegas. Like is that a good hook? Maybe not for you, but for your dream customer? Right, that's an amazing hook. Here's how to have all the things you want in life without any of the things you don't want in life. So it's like we're throwing out hooks and we're testing hooks all the time. Hook after hook after hook after hook after hook to see what things grab people's attention. And so the next thing is you're building out an inventory of hooks, like what are all the different hooks I can have? Every time we create a new product, like when the, here we go. This is the new Traffic Secrets book. Well, it's an image of it, it's not an actual book. But as we've been creating this book, I've been taking pictures of myself, writing the book, pictures of myself, editing the book, pictures of us, taking the shots for the cover, videos of that, we got, me speaking in vents about this. Like there's me writing this book in my kid's car when I feed up and I watch my daughter play soccer. There's me and my kids in the swimming pool writing the book. I've been documenting everything in this journey because all those things will turn into ads as soon as this book goes live. Every single thing you're doing, we're creating hook after hook after hook. And so it's like, as you're creating stuff, you start documenting the process of everything you're doing because every single thing you're doing can become a hook. So again, hooks hard to identify. It could be an image, it could be a video, it could be a headline, it could be all these things. But the biggest key is it's something that's gonna grab their attention just long enough. You can tell them a story, increase the perceived value, and then make the offer. So my question for you is how many hooks are you throwing out? My guess is you need to throw out a whole bunch more, okay? All right, so hook, story, and offer, that is the bait section here in the secret formula. Now remember, with hook, story, offer, every single page in your funnel has hook, story, offers, okay, on your ad, there's a hook, there's a story, there's an offer. And then they click, then they come to you landing page. There's a hook, there's a story, there's an offer. Then they go to your sales page, there's a story, there's an offer, they go to your upsell page, there's hook, story, and offer. It's in every single element, everything you do, every email you send out, there's a hook, story, offer, every podcast you publish, there's a hook, story, offer, Every video, everything you're doing, there's a hook, a story, and offer. If it's not working, it's always one of those things. Either your hook was wrong, your story was wrong, or your offer's no good. If you want to become amazing at this game, it's become amazing at understanding this and figuring out how do I make my hooks sexier? How do I increase my offer? How do I make my stories better? The better you get those three things, the better you're going to be at everything in this entire journey. Does that make sense? This is the core fundamentals behind funilology, behind making your funnels work, is understanding these principles. If something's not working, it's always either your hook, your story, or your offer. Let's come back to the secret formula, and then we're going to take a break here in about two minutes. Beneath the gaze of the Nashville Sun, life at Funnel Hacking Live begins to take form. A sight not to be missed. Here we can see the migration of nearly 5,000 Funnel Hackers descend upon an unsuspecting hotel. For a brief period of four days, this habitat will dramatically change. The Funneless Hackers form is a rare species, but ever growing by the thousands each passing day. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings, rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment, and of course, recording more acts because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro-yoga, and handshakes from the field to the bush. In the early hours, they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race towards seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there, they revel at the arrival of the alphas who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time, their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom. As the voyage comes to an end, they celebrate with a multitude of unpolished dance rituals, and whatever this is, a pleasing sensation. They have replenished their minds and spirits and are fit to return to the wild. FHL is a unique anthropological phenomenon, fit for the most peculiar of entrepreneurs. Welcome back to module number two of the Funnelology training course, the new art and science of funnel building. I hope you guys enjoyed the first module, and that you've got a lot of ideas so far to help you understand the core concepts, some of these core fundamental concepts that will help you to build, understand funnels, right? Like who is your dream customer? Where are they congregating? How do you create a hook a story offer to grab those people? And the next step is how do we get those people results? And so that's what this whole session is about. Now you've got this person. You've done all the work, right? You figure exactly who they were. You found where they were at. You hooked them. You got their attention. You told them a story. You made them an offer, and they're like excited. Now what? Now what most businesses do, unfortunately, is they sell somebody something, and then they hide like, uh, I hope the person doesn't see me again, right? That's not the way it should be. After somebody comes into your world, this is the beginning of a relationship, not the end. That's the difference between businesses that are successful and businesses that fail. Businesses that fail are ones who are their transaction. Like, I got your money. I'm out. I got your money. I'm out. I don't ever want to be in a business that's transactional. I want to be in a business that's relationship based, right? Somebody comes in, they buy something, and I'm going to give them value. They're going to enjoy the relationship. And if I do it right, they can continue to follow me on this path. Because I mean, you think about like the goal of business is not so much to make money as much as to change somebody's life. What's the result? What's the product, the service, the things you can do to change that person's life? And we start looking at through that lens, it shifts everything. So it'll make more sense as we go a little deeper in here how that all works. But so I'm going to jump right in. So session number two again is called the value ladder. This is, again, a core concept I taught initially in the dot com seekers book. And it's so fundamental behind everything we talk about inside a funnel building. Understanding this core concept will make everything else simpler as we kind of go through this. So a couple core principles to understand before we dive in. This is my mentor, Dan Kennedy, one of my initial mentors and someone who I have tons of respect for. To this day, everybody's stuff I've ever studied and I continue to study, I still spend the most time with Dan. And Dan says, I'm amazing. He said, ultimately the business that can spend the most money to acquire a customer wins. So everything we're talking about in this session is all based on this principle. Understanding that. Ultimately the business that can spend the most money to acquire a customer wins. So that's the job. That's the goal. That's what we're trying to do here. And funnels give you the ability to spend way more money to acquire a customer. But before you have a funnel, you have to figure out where are you taking people. And so before we get to funnels, we start mapping out this piece of it, which is called the value ladder. Now let me walk you through this process. So this is how the value ladder works. So this right here is value. This right here is price. If you look at these accesses here, I started thinking about my dream customers. So I want to start with this. If you could really transform and change somebody's life at the highest level, and if money didn't matter, what really matters is that you're going to get somebody and you're going to change their life, what would that look like? And think about me and the funnel world. The way that I could do that is, let's say, the way that I could change life the most is, let's say, I found you. And I say, I'm going to have you fly to Boise. So I pay for a flight. You fly over here to Boise. You get here. My entire team, I'm going to sit here, and we're going to film your product up here. And then my team is going to design your funnel. We're going to drive traffic. We're doing everything for you. That would be the best possible thing we could offer somebody. And so that's at the top of the value. So the value is really, really high. This is the most insane value ever where Russell does the entire thing for you. No, obviously, if I'm going to do that, the problem is it's going to cost a lot, because I've got to pay for your flights, and your hotel, and then the space, and my team to film videos, and edit videos, and to build your funnel, and to write your copy. All the different pieces that go into that. So it's going to cost a lot of money. So the value is high, but the price is high as well. But in a perfect world, this is what I would sell everybody. That's why I could serve somebody at the highest level. So that's kind of the beginning part of the value ladder, understanding that. And I always tell people, imagine if you just saw me for the first time ever. We just met. Hi, I'm Russell Brunson. I know I look like I'm 11 years old, but I'm actually this funnel nerd, and I'm really good at things like that. If you pay me $1 million plus 30% of all sales comes through your funnel, I will build you a funnel. I'll fly out here. My team will do all the work. What do you think? You'd be like, wait, what? Did you just say $1 million? No, no, no. $1 million plus 30% of all revenue you ever make from that funnel, or any lead that you sell after that funnel. That's what I charge for this. You'd be like, you're insane. You look like you're 11 years old. I might give you $1 million cash that hopefully you'll build me a funnel that might work, right? Because it's too high. Most people are going to say no to that offer, because why? Because so far, we haven't received any value. Like, you have no relationship. Like, why would I pay this person $1 million? This makes no logical sense, right? So in a perfect world, I would just sell my best thing. In reality, that's hard because of that, right? Because the price is so high. So what we do instead is we build what we call a value ladder. So the way a value ladder works is I start down here, and I offer something where the value is good, but the price is low, right? So this is like a small price. This is like your big price. It's expensive. And price, value goes up here as well. So over here, let's just say I came to you and I said, hey, you should buy a copy of my book. It's called dot com secrets. It's amazing, right? It's this at the bottom of the value ladder. And you're like, OK, that looks awesome. I'm like, oh, it is. Trust me. OK, so this is a book. The thing is, it's free. It just covers like $7.95 shipping handling. So the price is low. The value is good. You get the book in the mail. You're reading. You're like, oh, my gosh, this is so great. I want like, this book's amazing. I got so much value from this. Then guess what happens inside your mind? You're like, that is awesome. I got so much value. I want more. What else does Russell have? And so you start looking. You're like, what else does Russell have? And also, you start looking. And as humans, we always do this. We naturally ascend up when we want more stuff. You start looking. Well, this book was good. What else does Russell have? And so you say, well, the next step is he's got this over here. It's more expensive. It's more expensive here, but you get more value. So maybe this is like, you get a home study course. So say, home study course. So you go through home study course. You go through. You're like, that was amazing. That was awesome. You're like, well, what's the next tier? And you're like, oh, that was good. You should come to Funnel Hacking Live, because the price is more expensive, but there's more values. Come to FHL. And you can go through this tier. And eventually, you get a spot where you're like, I'm going to give Russell a million dollars. Because I've provided value in every single step along this value ladder. And so when you get there, you're like, man, I made this with somebody here. This is somebody here. All along the way, they're more likely to just say yes to your hiring offers. You think about this. Just human psychology. This isn't just true in business. This is true in all things in life. I know in session one, I gave you as an awkward example of me dating, which was really awkward for me to explain. Probably worse for you guys to listen to. But I'm going to give you another one. So imagine the value ladder when you meet someone for the very first time. So I met my wife. And I was like, dang, she is cute. If I'd walked up initially and be like, hey, do you want to get married and have babies? She'd be like, what? Like, you're a creep. And she would have ran away, right? So I didn't do that, luckily. Instead, I was like, OK, I'm making her offer. Collette, I want to take you on a date. And if we do on this date, here's the offer. And I'd create this offer, the whole bunch of value I'd tell it to her. And she's like, all right, I'll go on a date with you. Sweet. Right? She took the no-brainer offer here. The Quilter of Ika book. Like, I'll give you $7. I'll give you $10 to try to book. Maybe it's good. We'll find out. She's going on this date. We have an amazing time. It's a great experience. And then what happens naturally? If I get value, she gets value. We'll have value this thing. Then naturally, she's going to want more. And I'll be like, hey, that was really fun. She's like, I did have a good time. Like, hey, do you want to go on a date next week? She's like, yeah, I would love to. And all of a sudden, it moves to the next step, right? Then maybe the next week on a date. And then the doorstep, I give her a kiss. And then we both get value. Like, that was awesome. And it goes to the next thing. And eventually, somewhere down the line, I say, hey, do you want to get married? Let's have a bunch of kids together. And she's like, I would love to. And we get married. Now we've got five kids together. But I didn't lead with that, right? You lead down here. You bring somebody in. You provide value. And people naturally want to send up your value ladder. So the same way it happens in relationships is the same way it happens in business. That's what I core to understand. So that's what the value ladder is. We're taking somebody on this journey. Somebody comes in, like from last module. They see the hook, the story, they off. They come in, they get the bait. They buy the book. We go on a first date, whatever the equivalent is for your business. They come into your clinic. And you adjust them. Or they come in for a teeth cleaning. Like, something happens. They come in. Now it's like, we've started the relationship. Again, this is not the end. So many transactional businesses think that this is the business. This is the end. And it's like, no, this is the beginning. This is the beginning of the relationship. Now it's like, how do I provide value? How do I take them up here? And so to do this correctly, you start thinking about your business a little bit differently. And that's why I'm going to go deep into here with this whole value ladder presentation. So here's kind of a doodle of what a value ladder looks like. Now I'm going to share some examples of value ladders I've been through. And I want you to, as I tell you, to start thinking about it. And the better you get used to understanding things, the more you can start seeing them all the time. Whenever I tell people this first time, it's like a magic trick. And then they're like, I get messaged on Instagram and Facebook. Like, Russell, I just went to the coolest value ladder today. They check this out. I'm like, oh, that's amazing. We all geek out together. It's fun. So the first time I ever saw this was right after my wife and I just got married. I started this company. And at the time, it was just me. And I was selling stuff. And then I was like, I need a hired employee. So I hired my first employee, my second employee. And then we had a handful of people. And they asked me, hey, do we get benefits? I'm like, I don't know what that means. I think the benefit of hanging out with me is pretty awesome, right? And they're like, no, that's not awesome. I'm like, what do you mean benefits? And they're like, well, in a real company, we get dental insurance and health insurance, things like that. And I'd never had a job before. So I didn't know how that works. I was like, oh, we figured out. So eventually, we figured out a way to get dental insurance for everyone. That was our first big benefit. So we got dental insurance. And then I got a postcard in the mail. It was like, hey, coming for a free teeth cleaning. I was like, oh my gosh. So this dentist had a postcard. So free teeth cleaning. I got in the mail. I was like, OK, I have dental insurance now. I got free teeth cleaning. I'm in. So I went to the dentist. Went for my free teeth cleaning. Took the card in there. They went and cleaned my teeth. I was receiving value. I'm like, this is really, really great. And as I'm getting my teeth cleaned, I remember the dentist asked me something. Or maybe it was a hygienist. I can't remember exactly. It's been a little while. But the basic answer is, so do you smoke or do you drink coffee? I was like, no. Why? Like, oh, well, your teeth were kind of yellow. So I assumed maybe you were a smoker or you drank coffee. I'm like, what? My teeth are yellow? I'm like, yeah, but that's OK. If you want, we have a teeth cleaning kit. We could give it to you. And you can clean your teeth. And I'm like, oh, yes. Please, like, yes. Give me a teeth cleaning click, right? Teeth cleaning kit. And so they told me about that. I said, yes, give me the thing. They upsold me. Boom. And I got the teeth cleaning kit, right? I moved up the value ladder. I spent more money. Price went up. And I got more value. Then they're cleaning a little while. I said, hey, did you have braces on your kid? I'm like, yeah, I did. How can you tell? Like, oh, well, your bottom teeth are crowding. Your top are kind of shifting a little bit. I was like, wait, what? Like, yeah, but don't stress about it. If you want, there's an invisible line. There's retainers, things like that. We can do it. I'm like, well, tell me about that. Tell me about those kind of things. And eventually, back then, I got a retainer. Recently, I got an invisible line to help straighten out my teeth, right? And boom, there's like a, I don't even know how much it cost. $3,000 thing there. And did I get mad? It's like, no, the price went up, but I would see more value. Now I got white teeth. I got straight teeth. And luckily for me, I didn't have to have cosmetic dentistry. That's like the next step, right? You get cosmetic dentistry, and you move people up this value ladder. So if you look at the typical value ladder for a dentist, here's right here, right? Comes with teeth cleaning. There's whitening. There's retainer and invisible line. There's cosmetic stuff. And they have a continuity program built in as well, where every single six months, you come back for new teeth cleaning, new teeth cleaning, new teeth cleaning. So this is the value ladder of a dentist. And this is a pretty established thing, right? There are more advanced markets that understand these principles inherently. And they have them set up, and they do them. And dentists are one of the great examples of that, right? They do it over and over and over again. So that's the value ladder for a dentist. And I want to start, you start thinking about different value ladders that you've been through. Here's the value ladder for an author, speaker, coach, consultant. You've probably seen this live. You've come in into my world at all, or other speakers, or authors, or things that you've tried to lead. A lot of times, they come in with a free book offer. Then they send it to a home study offer. Then maybe there's a live event, and there's a mastermind, and there's a suspension that goes up, where the value goes up and the price goes up. One of my favorite examples of this are Stacey and Paul Martino. They're some of my inner circle members who recently moved to Boise, Idaho, which is awesome. And Stacey actually spoke at last Funnel Hacking Live about value ladder. And I had her speak because they have one of the best value ladders I've seen in our community, but also she had a really unique way of teaching value ladder. She called her value ladder the Yellowbrick Road. And she said, if you think about it, she said, every single customer who comes to you, they have an awe that they want. You guys are here listening to me because you want to grow your company through Funnels. You want to make more money. You want to get more customers. That's your awe. So the value ladder is me taking you on this journey to the Wizard of Oz. And so she's talking about the Yellowbrick Road, and then she showed hers. And this is like their Yellowbrick Road. If you look at this image, it's so awesome. It starts down here with Dorothy, and then it moves up to Glinda the Witch, and then the people on the journey. And this whole thing taking their customer to Oz. So this is kind of their past she showed at Funnel Hacking Live. And then you check this out. This is their company. They come in, and there's a free web class. They move to their relationship breakthrough quick start, and then their retreat, and then their level one, level two, level three, level four. This is their Yellowbrick Road to Oz. So I kind of doodled it out so you can see it inside of our context. This is their relationship development value ladder. And right now, they just recently launched a new tier of the value ladder, a free tier, that moved down a little bit. They dropped down. Their podcast is free offers. From there, it sends people up to their fourth day boost challenge. And from there, they quick start home study. And then from their relationship breakthrough retreat, and from their relationship coaching program. So they're sending people through this relationship. It's not a transactional business for someone that comes in once and they leave. They come and say, look, you're a customer of Oz now. We want to serve you at the highest level. Like, what else can we do? What else can we do? What else can we do? This is why it's so vitally important in the session we did session number one, talking about finding your dream customer, and finding somebody that you love to be around, that you're obsessed with, you want to serve them at all levels. Because if you get the right person, this part of your business becomes so much fun. Every morning, you wake up like, how is this going to serve me? How is this going to serve them? This whole entire training we're doing right now all came out of a question of the New Year's starting. A lot of our community is struggling still with the core fundamental principles of funnel building. Like, what do we do? Let's do a course called Funalology. It'll be amazing. We'll do it live over three days, then we'll turn to a course later and like, and boom, now we're here. Because it's always with me being obsessed with you guys. My dream customers, people actually care about. So for Stacy and Paul, they're helping people that have relationship problems. They've got a really cool process. Those of you who don't know, they help save over 10,000 marriages. And they do it in a way that's very unique. It's not couples counseling. In fact, they say that if you go into counseling, the two people involved, it always falls apart. It's where a relationship transformation where just one person's involved. So they bring people in to come listen to their podcast, and they listen like, oh my gosh, this is amazing. They tell their own stories. They break false beliefs. They break false epiphanies. They provide value. And they're like, hey, if you like this stuff, come jump into our 14-day Boost Challenge. Boom, they come up. And then they're thinking, how else can we serve these people? We love these people. We want to help their marriage. We want to help their relationships. What else do we do? Oh, let's make a home study program. Oh, let's do a live event. And they kept creating these things to send people up towards Oz. So that's the power of value ladder, you guys. When you get the right dream customer, the value ladder becomes fun. It's just, how else can we serve these people? How can we change their life? What's their Oz? The more you think about what's the Oz in my customer, the more this becomes easier. And I'm like, oh, well, this is the process I'm going to be taking people through. This is my buddy's Chad Wulner. He's a chiropractor. And when I first met Chad, his business was kind of struggling. And it was funny because we were always talking about marketing and funnels and all these kind of things. He's like, well, how does work in my practice? And I started going just to see how his old process worked. And a customer would see an ad. They'd come to the clinic. He would do adjustment, like a $50 adjustment. It takes like three minutes to adjust somebody and then send them home. And hopefully, the person would come back next week and get another adjustment. That's kind of the process of their business. I was like, huh, that's what chiropractors do, I guess. I didn't really know. I'd never been around chiropractors before I really met Chad. And so that was kind of the business as I knew it. And I remember about the time, this is probably before the London Olympics, I had a crazy hair. And I decided I wanted to try to compete for the Olympics. And so instead of doing normal people do and plan things out, I called up the Olympic Greco wrestling coach. And I offered him a job, moved him to Boise, Idaho, bought a facility, hired all the guys who were wrestling on the Olympic Greco team, and flew them to Boise. Gave them housing and shelter and gave them jobs. And I was like, all right, now I can compete for the Olympics. I got the whole team here. And so we started competing, and it was really fun. And we did it for about seven or eight months. And there's a whole other backdoor behind that of why I never made it to the Olympics and why we ended up shutting it down. But in the interim, when we were all training, and I was training and trying to figure things out, we used to have a chiropractor who'd come every single day after practice and adjust all the wrestlers. And I remember one day, the chiropractor, she wasn't able to make it. And she called the guys, like, hey, I can't come in, because of whatever, whatever thing. And I was like, oh, no worries. And so Justin Reese, when the guys on the wrestling team, he literally opened up YouTube and started watching YouTube videos, like how to do a chiropractic adjustment. And he watched two or three videos. And he got done. He's like, OK, I know how to do it. And he got all the guys lined up, and he started adjusting everyone on the team. And I'm watching the mazes. I'm like, first off, is he going to kill these people? Like, is he going to break their neck? Like, he's a big, you know, he's the third in the world. He's a guy who's like a big, huge, probably one of the strongest, scariest men you'll ever meet if you met him on the street. Also, like, the nicest guy ever. But like, he's adjusting all these people. I'm like, we're either going to have someone, you know, going to have some problems or something's going to happen. But he adjusts everybody. Everyone's fine afterwards. And remember that night I called up, I called up Chad, Dr. Wollner. I was like, dude, this is the funniest thing. And I knew he was going to be mad. But I told him, I was like, Justin's watched a video about how to adjust people. And he adjusted everybody. And I was kind of laughing. And he got so mad. He was like, first off, he shouldn't be adjusting some of that on education. Like, all these things. I'm like, no, no, no, I totally get you. You were completely right. He was stupid. You should not have done that. But dude, I want to point something out to you real quick. The only thing that you sell right now, Justin was able to learn in about 10 minutes on YouTube. And now he's able to do it as well. I said, when you were in medical school doing all the stuff that you did to become a chiropractor, did you learn anything else besides how to do that one little thing? He's like, yeah, we learned how to do this and this. And like, nutritional things and how to do this. And like, I had never all these things. It's going on and on and on and on. And after about five minutes of spilling, all the stuff you learned that he was capable of doing, I stopped him. I said, then why are you only selling one thing? Why are you only selling adjustment? What other value could you actually provide to your dream customers when they come in the door? He's like, oh my gosh. I never thought about that before. We started talking about it. Like, well, what else could you do? Like, well, I could make a wellness program. I could do this. I could start looking like, what could you create up here? What could you create down here? And also shifted from like, all we do is we sell adjustments. This is a typical chiropractic value that I always sell as adjustments to like, what other value can provide your dream customers? They're coming to you. They're trying to get relief. What's their odds? They're trying to get healthier, trying to get happier, trying to lose weight, trying to gain weight. What's the odds for your dream customers coming in? Now, let's create different products, different things on the value ladder to help them to get to their end goal. And so from that, Chad created a whole bunch of things. He's created free information, a massage, health assessments, wellness programs, a whole bunch of things. He built out a whole value ladder. And now, Chad's able to spend more money to acquire a customer than any of the other chiropractors in town. Because now he's not just selling $50 adjustment. He also sells a $5,000 thing and a $2,000 thing and a whole bunch of other things as well. See how that works? So that's the process of the value ladder. So the question is, what does your value ladder look like? The first thing I want to talk about, to help you understand this, is talking about what your value ladder look like, is you got to figure out the odds for your customer. We're always thinking about what's the product we're going to sell, what's the offer we're going to make. And it's like, those are parts of what this game is about. But the real game is, what's the odds? What's the end story? What's the big result we're trying to get for somebody? And so we do that by creating what we call a value ladder mission statement, VLMS. This was not in the original dot com secrets book. But as we started building more companies over the last five or six years, we found out how vitally important this was. And now it's in the new version you guys will see when you have a chance to read it. But what a value ladder mission statement is, is this is the mission statement of your company. A lot of times people will come to me and they want me to invest in their business and they'll send me, here's my business plan. It's like 800 pages of just fluff about like, oh, the industry bubble and all these things. I'm like, can you doodle it on an app kit? And if it doesn't look like a value ladder, you should probably just leave. This is your business model for me. Someone will come in and then we're going to send them here and here and here. And then the whole thing is the whole, it's like your value ladder mission statement is like, what's the end goal of this whole thing? If I'm in business trying to sell people something, make money, I'm not in a good business. If I'm in a business to help people to do something, to get to a certain odds, that's the kind of business that actually has meaning and value in the world beyond just making transactions. So this is what the value ladder mission statement looks like. We help somebody, our dream customers, we help our dream customers to get this result through a certain vehicle, through your specific vehicle. So if I was a dentist, I'd say something like this. We help families in our community, guess our dream customers, to increase their confidence, that's the result they want, through creating a beautiful smile they're proud to share. Can be an example of a value ladder mission statement. So everything we now do inside of our business comes back to that. Is this offer, is it helping families in our community increase their confidence through a beautiful smile? In this line? Yes, it does, it makes troll sense. This fits inside our value ladder. A lot of these people, app things are value ladder, but I think they put in there because it's gonna make more money, it's like this doesn't fit into your mission statement of your value ladder. This is the mission statement of your whole company. If it doesn't fit in this thing, you gotta get rid of it. In fact, this year you probably noticed we've cut a lot of things out of click funnels, things software features, coaching programs, the whole bunch of stuff that we did in the past we got rid of, because it wasn't fueling the value ladder mission statement. So here's the value ladder mission statement for click funnels. We help, who are my dream customers? Visionary entrepreneurs, those are my dream customers. We help them do what? The odds is to grow their companies. They can grow their companies a lot of different ways. They can do it through different channels or vehicles, but I want them to grow their companies through my specific vehicle, which is a sales funnel. So I help visionary entrepreneurs to grow their companies through sales funds. That's the value ladder mission statement of my entire value ladder. So as I'm designing any product or program or service or software or everything comes back to like, does it fit into the mission statement of our company? If not, then it doesn't fit. There's not a point, there's not a purpose for it. So we fulfill our value ladder mission statement through a whole bunch of stuff, through books, online courses, live events, through software, through coaching, through mastermind groups. These are all things we do to help people to achieve our value ladder mission statement. If you look at our value ladder right now, it's very simple. I've had times in the past where my value ladder were super complex. We've called them value mazes sometimes, where even customers get lost going through different channels from places. Over the last year, we've been simplifying and chopping and simplifying and chopping and simplifying and chopping. And for the most part, in fact, not all this has been released yet, but if I'm not gonna lie this year, the last step will be released. But this is our value ladder now. On the bottom tier, we've got a whole bunch of books. Dotcom Secrets, Expert Secrets, Traffic Secrets, Lead Phone, like all of our front end books that people come in for a very low ticket, they receive value. If they like that, then we try to get them on the next step to, hey, if you thought Dotcom Secrets was good, you should try our software. It's called ClickFunnels. It's amazing. It's 97 bucks a month. Use this to build your funnels. If they like ClickFunnels, our next goal is to send them up to the next one, which is ClickFunnels Platinum. And a lot of you guys are in ClickFunnels Platinum. It's $297 a month. You get ClickFunnels, but you get an unlimited account of ClickFunnels, plus you get funnel flicks and all that training. It's the next level, next tier. So that's the next tier. If you go there, you get a bunch of value, and you're like, this is amazing. Got all this training, all this programs, all this everything, plus all the software upgrades. Then hopefully you'll send it to the next level, which is called ClickFunnels Collective, which is not live yet, so I can't tell you anything else about it. But it's amazing, I promise you that. But it's simple. Your value ladder is gonna be 8,000 steps. Ours has four, and we're a company that's well over $100 million a year. Simple value ladders that you can take people through. All right, so your value ladder is your business plan. As you look at this, your value ladder comes in, there's free offers, there's fronted offers, there's middle value ladder offers, and there's back end offers. Now I'm gonna go a little deeper in this because for each tier of the value ladder, there's different kind of funnels you use. I'm gonna talk about it in a minute. But the first thing I wanna, this is the caveat that I'm gonna talk about value ladders I get a little nervous about, is I show people this, and they're like, oh my gosh, this is a total sense. So what that means is I'm gonna create a huge coaching program. I'm gonna create some live event and a home study course and a book, and they get there and they're like, this is everything I create, this is my business model. And they're like, oh my gosh, that's a lot of stuff to create. And they go and they try to launch all these things at once. And they're creating coaching programs and mastermind groups and home study courses and events and everything, and they never get out of the door. The value ladder is your business plan. This is where we're going. This is not where you are today. You can ask any people that are in my inner circle for the last five or six years as I was running it. I always told people, I said, look, when you come in inner circle, you're allowed to pick one, one tier of the value ladder, and you're allowed to only focus on that one offer that one funnel helps made at least a million dollars. After it's made a million dollars, then you're allowed to do funnel number two. Okay, because all of them have the same problem, guessing you do as well. What happens? They get all excited, like, this is amazing. And they build a funnel, they make like, they launch it, they make like 30 grand or 100 grand. Like, this is awesome. They make another funnel, they make another funnel, they launch it, they make 20, 30 grand. They make another one, they can do it, they can do it, and then companies never grow. Okay, and so for all of them, I came in, like most people at first came in the room, first thing we do is we kill 90% of their funnels, say, focus on just one, and then that's the only one you're allowed to focus on until it's made a million dollars. As soon as you get to Comic Club Award, then you can do your second funnel, but not until it's hit to Comic Club. And they're always like, fill handcuffed at first, and then free later on, and realize, oh my gosh, I just gotta be creative and figure out more ways to sell this. So that's the big key is that, for all you guys, we're not gonna have you do all of these. You're gonna pick one, and you're gonna focus on that until it hits to Comic Club, then you're allowed to do number two. So this is the business plan. This is saying, I know I'm going here right now, but where do I start? For most people, I recommend starting in the middle of the value ladder. And I wanna show you guys what those look like here in a second, but it's easier to start there. I mean, something that's like $500, maybe $2,000, something like that, selling through a webinar, or a presentation, or I'll show you the funnels here in a little bit, but starting there in the middle, how that'd be your first funnel, and your focus to your first million bucks is usually the best. Then for example, when we launched ClickFunnels, guess what we did? We had a $1,000 version of ClickFunnels. I went out, I did a webinar, at least one a week, usually three or four a week, every single week for an entire year, until we'd done over $10 million in sales, and then I went down, and I launched the Dotcom Seekers book. I didn't launch them all at once, for so many reasons. One is then your ad team's like driving ads this, and this, and this, and it's just everything is watered down, and you don't succeed, because you're not focused. If you focus on one funnel to hit some million bucks, that's the most likely to be successful. So don't get stressed out of looking at my value ladder, and be like, I'm gonna create all this crap. Look at it and say, this is the future over the next five years, what I'm gonna create. For the next year, I'm just creating this one. I'm creating a webinar, I'm creating a course, and I'm creating whatever, and I'm gonna focus all my energy, all my effort on that one funnel, gonna focus on it. Rust keeps saying, I'm one funnel away, I'm one funnel away, I'm one funnel away. Maybe he knows what he's talking about, maybe he's coached thousands of people to the Two Comic Club, maybe I'm just gonna pay attention to him, and just focus on the one, tell you to Two Comic Club, and then it's like, I have all this built up, all this built up like demand for something now is gonna release next thing. Liz Benny's an example of this. When Liz first came into our world, she came in right before Click Phone's launch. She was one of our very first people ever on the platform. Still one of my favorite people of this day. And Liz launched her very first webinar, and she focused on it, and she sold it, and she sold it, and she sold this $1,000 course, until she got over a million dollars, and she passed Two Comic Club, and I was like, okay, now, what do you wanna create now? She's like, my people are freaking out. I'm like, what do they want? She's like, they want a coaching program, and I'm like, how do you know? She's like, cause they keep yelling at me every single day, and I said, yes, now's the time you release the backend of your value ladder. There's so much built up demand and attention. People want it, and it's easy to get like, hey, I got a $25,000 coaching program. Who wants in? And boom, overnight, you do more money in one email than you did the entire year, prior year to it, right? I remember Justin Terry Williams on the real estate market, and same thing, they had a $2,000 or $3,000 course. They're selling like crazy, and then they came to our first-year circle meeting, and I said, you guys should launch a $25,000 course. And remember, they looked at me and said, well, nobody would buy a $25,000 course. And I was like, how much did you spend to be in this room? And at the time, in their circle, it was $25,000, and I came over to Justin Terry, I was like, oh, we paid $25,000. I'm like, okay, so you paid for it, so now you just gotta believe other people pay you for it. And so that night, they went to the hotel room, they sent out an email, and I think they got 16 people that paid $25,000 from the one email. But if they were to just start with that, they emailed their non-existing list to run Facebook ads to a $25,000 program, it probably wouldn't have happened. Why? Cause people, it's going straight to here. They didn't build up any value yet. You start something like this, you build up the value, then you're like, oh, here's a coaching program, these people will go crazy for it. That's why I always tell people, when you start your company, it's like, like you have a big boulder, and you're pushing it up a mountain, and you're pushing and you're pushing, it's like harder, it's like harder, harder, harder. So you get the top of the mountain, then it goes over the edge, then it starts taking off like crazy, right? Hardest thing is getting your first two comic books, cause you're putting in all the effort, you're pushing this thing, you sell, you have to sell, but you're at the top, you got a thousand customers that paid you $1000. That's like, here's a coaching program, and boom, overnight, 30 people paid 25 grand. It's like, whoo, takes off, and then the next thing you sell, the next thing you sell becomes really, really easy. So from zero to million bucks is the hardest part. After a million, it's easier and easier and easier as you start growing your company. The hardest part is this phase right here. But so that's why you gotta focus here. If you're trying to do this and launch this and write a book and do this all the same time, none of them are gonna work. So one funnel till you pass a million bucks. Now, when do you come back and start adding these other things? What happens is eventually, if I have, let's say it's a webinar funnel or a presentation funnel, and I'm driving all the ads to it, eventually you start getting ad fatigue. Where we had that same thing, we sold over $10 million in click funnels, and the ads started fatiguing because so many people seen the ads. So the bottom of the value ladder, I'm trying to get stuff here, I call these lead funnels. These are funnels where I'm getting leads to come into me. Now there's a lot of types of lead funnels. You're saying, give me your email address and I will give you this thing. That's the offer, right? Okay, so that's the first tier. We move to the next tier. This is, I call them an unboxing funnel. So for example, the three unboxing funnels that 90% of people use, again, I have no idea, but it's pretty much the majority of people who use one of these three funnels for the unboxing funnels. Here we can see the migration of nearly 5,000 Funnel Hackers descend upon an unsuspecting hotel. For a brief period of four days, this habitat will dramatically change. The Funneless Hacker Form is a rare species, but ever growing by the thousands each passing day. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings, rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment, and of course recording more acts, because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro yoga and handshakes from the field to the bush. In the early hours they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race towards seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there they revel at the arrival of the alphas, who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom. As the voyage comes to an end, they celebrate with a multitude of unpolished dance rituals and whatever this is, a pleasing sensation. They have replenished their minds and spirits and are fit to return to the wild. FHL is a unique anthropological phenomenon, fit for the most peculiar of entrepreneurs.
